cases.answer:
Here is a summary of the news in 83 words:

Wardi McDonald, 34, disappeared from Detroit in 2001 without a trace. Her last known whereabouts were with her siblings, but no information is available about her personal or professional life. Despite being classified as an endangered missing person, little is known about Wardi, including her physical appearance, clothing, or any distinguishing features. Any information can be reported to the Detroit Police Department.

contexts.text:
Wardi McDonald, 34: Last Seen By Her Siblings In 2001
This is another “one-sentence” case that has been on the books for over 20 years. When I say “one sentence,” I mean that in any report I could find, there is only one sentence to explain the case's circumstances. There are thousands of these within databases, especially those involving Black women, as details are either unknown or the authorities haven’t shared any information that could help with the investigation. The story of Wardi McDonald reads like several others, as she vanished from Detroit, Michigan, on February 1, 2001.
It has already been established that it isn’t much known about this case, but it has been shared that the 34-year-old’s siblings were the last to see her. Where she was, who she may have been with, if she was traveling to or from another location, or any information about her personal or professional life remains a mystery. Sadly, nothing has been revealed about Wardi besides her physical attributes that could help her identify.
Wardi is listed as an endangered missing person, but it is also unclear why the police decided on that classification.
At the time of her disappearance, Wardi McDonald stood 5 feet 3 to 5 feet 4 inches tall and weighed 120 to 130 pounds. She had brown hair with red/auburn tint kept short, and brown eyes. What she was last seen wearing is unknown. She reportedly has a mole on her nose and burns on half of her back. Wardi’s nickname is “Jackie,” and her middle name is “Jemilla.” Her dental records, fingerprints, and DNA are unknown. She would be 56 years old at the time of this publication.
Anyone with information about this case is urged to contact the Detroit Police Department at (313) 596-1800 or their local authorities. The agency case number is 1109260130.
Please share this story about Wardi McDonald to help reignite interest in her missing person case. She is our sister, and her life matters.

cases.answer: Texas Governor Greg Abbott has scored a victory as a judge temporarily halts border agents from removing concertina wire along the southern border. The decision comes after Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton sued the Biden administration over the issue. The judge's ruling allows for exceptions in cases of medical emergencies. The temporary relief will last until November 13, with arguments from both sides scheduled for November 7. Texas claims that border agents were cutting the wire, allowing migrants to freely enter the state, while the Biden administration argues it was done to provide medical aid.

contexts.text:
Governor Abbott Gets a Win as Judge Halts Border Agents Removing Wire
Governor Greg Abbott can thank President George W. Bush as one of his legal appointees supports a Texas issue. This comes after Attorney General Ken Paxton sued the Biden administration last week. U.S. Customs and Border Patrol agents were cutting concertina wire allowing migrants to freely cross the southern border.
On Monday, October 30, District Judge Alia Moses, granted Paxton’s request he filed last Tuesday.
Judge Moses said according to The Texas Tribune:
“The Court shall grant the temporary relief requested, with one important exception for any medical emergency that mostly likely results in serious bodily injury or death to a person, absent any boats or other life-saving apparatus available to avoid such medical emergencies prior to reaching the concertina wire barrier.”
The move by Moses is only temporary until November 13. Paxton’s office and the Biden administration will present their arguments over the concertina wire on November 7.
According to ABC News, Texas states border agents were "cutting, destroying, or otherwise damaging Texas’s concertina wire that had been strategically positioned for the purpose of securing the border and stemming the flow of illegal migration."
Texas states border agents cut through the wire allowing migrants to freely enter Texas.
In a statement, Paxton said according to CBS News:
“By acting quickly and monitoring their actions closely, we were able to secure a restraining order, and I am confident we will continue to prevail."
The Biden administration said the wire was cut so they could provide medical aid to migrants.
The Department of Homeland Security added according to CBS News:
"[Border agents] have a responsibility under federal law to take those who have crossed onto U.S. soil without authorization into custody for processing, as well as to act when there are conditions that put our workforce or migrants at risk."
How do you think the judge will rule on this case?

cases.note: Adapted from RAGTruth response 4339. Human review is required before using source-evidence span metrics because RAGTruth labels answer-side spans.

“By acting quickly and monitoring their actions closely, we were able to secure a restraining order, and I am confident we will continue to prevail."
The Biden administration said the wire was cut so they could provide medical aid to migrants.
The Department of Homeland Security added according to CBS News:
"[Border agents] have a responsibility under federal law to take those who have crossed onto U.S. soil without authorization into custody for processing, as well as to act when there are conditions that put our workforce or migrants at risk."
How do you think the judge will rule on this case?
output:

cases.answer:
Here is a summary of the article in 104 words:

Illinois Senate President Don Harmon has faced criticism over accepting large campaign contributions from the nursing home industry as he supports legislation that could provide substantial property tax breaks to nursing homes in Cook County. Critics argue that the contributions could have influenced Harmon's decision to support the tax relief measure, which was later vetoed by Governor J.B. Pritzker. Despite the controversy, the nursing home industry is seeking legislative support for an override of the veto.

contexts.text:
Illinois Senate President Backed by Nursing Home Industry Amid Tax Break Controversy
Campaign contributions raise questions about influence on tax legislation
Illinois Senate President Don Harmon finds himself at the center of a controversy as he supports legislation that could provide substantial property tax breaks to nursing homes in Cook County. The issue arises as it's revealed that he has accepted nearly $2 million in campaign contributions from the industry trade group representing nursing homes, with over $700,000 received in the past year.
The political action committee for the Health Care Council of Illinois has been one of Harmon's significant campaign contributors, advocating for the tax relief measure that would benefit nursing homes. Recent contributions from the group include $10,000 on October 19, $100,000 on September 30, and $5,000 on August 23. This support coincided with the General Assembly's passage of legislation backed by Harmon, which aimed to change the property tax assessment of private, for-profit nursing homes in Cook County.
The legislation would reclassify these nursing homes as residential property, lowering their property taxes by millions. In contrast, they are currently classified as commercial property in Chicago and inner-ring suburbs, subjecting them to higher real estate taxes.
Harmon has not provided a statement regarding the contributions or his stance on the legislation. However, his spokesman, John Patterson, emphasized that legislative decisions are made in the best interests of the public and to ensure fairness for all organizations.
Ron Nunziato, the senior director of policy and regulatory affairs at the Health Care Council, defended Harmon, noting his long-standing support for nursing homes. He claimed there was no direct link between the campaign contributions and Harmon's position on the legislation.
The controversy emerged in May when State Sen. Celina Villanueva filed an amendment that included the tax reclassification for Cook County nursing homes. While Patterson stated that Harmon's legislative staff was involved in drafting the provision, Villanueva didn't return calls.
Despite the controversy, the legislation passed the General Assembly and was sent to Governor J.B. Pritzker, who used an amendatory veto to remove the nursing home provision. Pritzker argued that the tax break would shift the financial burden onto suburban Cook County homeowners and small businesses, potentially causing financial hardships and foreclosure.
The Health Care Council and nursing homes in Cook County are now seeking legislative support for an override of the governor's veto. While it's unclear whether Harmon supports this move, a source familiar with the process indicated strong support from his Senate chief of staff, Jacob Butcher.

cases.note: Adapted from RAGTruth response 4348. Human review is required before using source-evidence span metrics because RAGTruth labels answer-side spans.

cases.answer: A former FBI agent and a D.C. real estate developer have been sentenced for their roles in a bribery scheme involving confidential data from the D.C. Department of Housing and Community Development. Ex-FBI agent David Paitsel was sentenced to 24 months in prison, while developer Brian Bailey received a 48-month sentence. The scheme involved payments made to an employee of the department in exchange for access to tenant data.

contexts.text:
Former FBI agent and D.C. real estate developer sentenced for bribery scheme
This article may contain AI-assisted content.
The US Attorney's Office in D.C. announced today that a former FBI special agent and a renowned D.C. real estate developer have been sentenced following their involvement in an extensive bribery scheme. The scheme was tied to the unauthorized sharing of confidential data from the D.C. Department of Housing and Community Development.
Key Points
Main Defendants
David Paitsel, 42, a former FBI agent from North Carolina
Brian Bailey, 53, a D.C. real estate developer from Upper Marlboro, Maryland
Bailey was convicted of paying significant sums to Dawne Dorsey, a program specialist with the D.C. Department of Housing and Community Development. In return, Dorsey provided Bailey with confidential and un-redacted Tenant Opportunity to Purchase Act (TOPA) offer of sale notices.
These TOPA notices are critical, offering D.C. tenants the right to buy their residence if an owner plans to sell. Some of these notices include private details not publicly available.
In another component of the bribery scheme, Bailey paid Paitsel to retrieve the contact information of tenants with TOPA rights. Paitsel had exclusive access to this data due to his position as an FBI agent.
Sentencing
Paitsel received a sentence of 24 months in prison on each count (to run concurrently), 24 months of supervised release, a $100 special assessment, and was ordered to pay a $10,600 forfeiture money judgment.
Bailey was handed a sentence of 48 months in prison on each count (to run concurrently), 24 months of supervised release, a $100 special assessment, and a substantial $250,000 fine.
Other Parties Involved
Dawne Dorsey, 40, had already pleaded guilty to her role in the bribery in June 2019. Her sentencing is set for November 6, 2023.
In a connected case, Frederick Silvers from Washington, D.C., admitted his guilt over bribery and received a 5-month prison sentence for bribes given to Dorsey.

cases.note: Adapted from RAGTruth response 4524. Human review is required before using source-evidence span metrics because RAGTruth labels answer-side spans.

cases.answer: Texas Governor Greg Abbott has condemned the recent attack on Israel and announced increased security measures to support Texas citizens. He denounced the act of war against Israeli citizens and Americans in Israel and expressed support for Israel's right to defend itself. Governor Abbott directed the Texas Department of Public Safety to provide support to potential targets and restricted Texas agencies from trading with the Gaza Strip if they have ties to Hamas. He also distributed $4 million in grant funding to enhance security at Jewish organizations.

contexts.text:
Texas Governor Abbott Condemns Attack on Israel, Increases Security
After Israel was attacked over the weekend, Texas Governor Greg Abbott denounced the war and showed support for Americans in the country. Governor Abbott announced several more initiatives to support Texas citizens.
On Monday, October 9, in a press release, Governor Abbott said:
“The State of Texas condemns these heinous acts of violence and inhumanity against Israel and its people by ruthless terrorists, and we stand ready to offer our complete support to the Israeli and Jewish communities.”
“I firmly denounce the act of war against Israeli citizens and Americans in Israel and support Israel’s right to defend itself from these barbaric attacks. Working with local partners across Texas, we are actively increasing security at Jewish and Israel-related locations such as synagogues and schools. In response to these cowardly attacks, we stand united with our Jewish neighbors and we repudiate terrorism and anti-Semitism."
Governor Abbott directed the Texas Department of Public Safety to provide support to local partners who may become targets as a result of the recent attack. He noted there may be attacks by Hezbollah or Hamas against the Jewish community in Texas.
In addition, Governor Abbott restricted all Texas agencies from purchasing or exporting goods to the Gaza Strip especially if they have ties to Hamas.
To provide additional safeguards, Governor Abbott distributed $4 million in grant funding to 31 Jewish organizations to increase their security.
Governor Abbott also directed the Texas flag to be flown at half-staff over the Israeli conflict and Americans who lost their lives in the country. The flag will remain at half-staff until this upcoming Saturday.
Earlier Governor Abbott posted the Executive Order supporting Israel on X.
According to CBS-19, over 1,600 people have lost their lives including Israeli, Palestinian, and American citizens.
Here is a recent update on the war:
What do you think the U.S. should do to support Israel?

cases.answer: California Governor Gavin Newsom, accompanied by First Partner Jennifer Siebel Newsom, will travel to China next week on a weeklong trip to focus on climate action and partnerships between the two countries. The visit aims to advance climate initiatives, promote economic development, boost tourism, and encourage cultural exchange. The trip is seen as crucial in solidifying the longstanding partnership between California and China, with a focus on transitioning to low-carbon economies. The itinerary includes visits to Hong Kong, Shenzhen, Guangzhou, Beijing, Shanghai, and the province of Jiangsu, where the Governor will engage in discussions and meetings with regional leaders, businesses, and government officials.

contexts.text:
California Gov. Newsom travels to China next week: Here's his jam-packed schedule in five cities
Governor Gavin Newsom, accompanied by First Partner Jennifer Siebel Newsom, is embarking on a weeklong trip to China, focusing on climate action and fostering partnerships between two of the world's largest economies, his office announced Wednesday.
The trip aims to advance climate initiatives, promote economic development, boost tourism, and encourage cultural exchange, according to the Governor's Office.
The visit precedes the crucial APEC Summit in San Francisco this November, amplifying the longstanding partnership between California and China, building on a solid bilateral foundation laid by previous Governors and Senators.
Governor Newsom's message during this trip is clear: the world's destiny hinges on climate action in California and China.
California and China, boasting the second and fifth-largest economies globally, are deeply intertwined. The visit seeks to solidify this relationship, emphasizing healthy economic competition while transitioning to low-carbon economies.
"California and China hold the keys to solving the climate crisis. As two of the world’s largest economies, our partnership is essential to delivering climate action for our communities and beyond. Our decades-long work together proves what we can accomplish together – cleaning the air, accelerating the transition to electric vehicles, protecting people from extreme weather, and conserving lands and oceans," remarked Governor Gavin Newsom.
The comprehensive itinerary covers prominent cities such as Hong Kong, Shenzhen, Guangzhou, Beijing, Shanghai, and the province of Jiangsu, showcasing various initiatives and partnerships.
HONG KONG
The Governor will engage in a fireside chat with Hong Kong University leadership, emphasizing shared efforts in combatting climate change while promoting robust economic growth.
GUANGDONG
In Guangdong, Governor Newsom will meet with regional leaders and businesses, highlighting the transformation of industrial cities into electric vehicle and public transit hubs.
The delegation will focus on China's accelerated transition to an all-electric bus fleet and the supporting infrastructure.
BEIJING
In Beijing, discussions will center on advancing climate collaboration, covering methane, the clean energy transition, economic growth, cultural ties, and combatting xenophobia.
The Governor will also meet high-level national and local officials, tour the Great Wall, and engage with stakeholders representing a significant portion of China's population.
JIANGSU
The Governor will visit an offshore wind facility and a wetlands preserve, examining China's efforts to become the world's largest wind power generator. California's ambitious offshore wind capacity goals will be a focal point of discussion.
SHANGHAI
Governor Newsom concludes the trip in Shanghai, signing the fifth MOU of the journey with local and subnational leadership.
The Governor will tour Tesla's Shanghai gigafactory.

cases.note: Adapted from RAGTruth response 4777. Human review is required before using source-evidence span metrics because RAGTruth labels answer-side spans.

The comprehensive itinerary covers prominent cities such as Hong Kong, Shenzhen, Guangzhou, Beijing, Shanghai, and the province of Jiangsu, showcasing various initiatives and partnerships.
HONG KONG
The Governor will engage in a fireside chat with Hong Kong University leadership, emphasizing shared efforts in combatting climate change while promoting robust economic growth.
GUANGDONG
In Guangdong, Governor Newsom will meet with regional leaders and businesses, highlighting the transformation of industrial cities into electric vehicle and public transit hubs.
The delegation will focus on China's accelerated transition to an all-electric bus fleet and the supporting infrastructure.
BEIJING
In Beijing, discussions will center on advancing climate collaboration, covering methane, the clean energy transition, economic growth, cultural ties, and combatting xenophobia.
The Governor will also meet high-level national and local officials, tour the Great Wall, and engage with stakeholders representing a significant portion of China's population.
JIANGSU
The Governor will visit an offshore wind facility and a wetlands preserve, examining China's efforts to become the world's largest wind power generator. California's ambitious offshore wind capacity goals will be a focal point of discussion.
SHANGHAI
Governor Newsom concludes the trip in Shanghai, signing the fifth MOU of the journey with local and subnational leadership.
The Governor will tour Tesla's Shanghai gigafactory.
output:

cases.answer: Marilyne Chamberlain, a 65-year-old former federal employee, disappeared in Maryland in 1998. She was last seen visiting a credit union and department store, and may have taken a metro ride after that. Despite investigations, her whereabouts remain unknown, and the case is considered suspicious. Chamberlain was described as 5'3" with distinguishable features, including pierced ears and a scar. Anyone with information can contact the Prince George's County Police Department at 301-352-1200 or their local police unit.

contexts.text:
Marilyne Chamberlain: The 65-years-old woman who vanished from Maryland without trace in 1998
Marilyne Chamberlain: A 1998 Disappearance Shrouded in Mystery
In the annals of unsolved mysteries, the perplexing disappearance of Marilyne Chamberlain from Maryland in 1998 continues to baffle investigators. Despite the passage of time, crucial details regarding the case remain scant, leaving the public and authorities grappling for answers.
Marilyne Chamberlain, 65 at the time of her disappearance, was a former federal employee, though specifics about her role or tenure remain undisclosed. On November 2, 1998, Chamberlain was last confirmed visiting the Federal Credit Union at the Pentagon in Washington, D.C., where she conducted a routine transaction. Eyewitnesses noted nothing unusual about her visit. Subsequently, she was spotted at a Sears and Roebuck department store, attending to her bills.
In what complicates the narrative, it's believed Chamberlain may have later been on the Washington Metropolitan Area Transit Authority subway, specifically in Landover Hills, Maryland. However, the exact sequence or timing of these events hasn't been fully ascertained. This Metro sighting marks the last known whereabouts of Chamberlain.
Although the trajectory of the police investigation remains under wraps, it's been hinted that foul play might factor into Chamberlain's disappearance. The context surrounding her case is undoubtedly tinged with suspicion. Any potential suspects or persons of interest have been kept out of public domain.
Marilyne, when last seen, stood at 5 feet 3 inches and weighed around 145 pounds. With a mix of dark brown, black, and gray hair complementing her brown eyes, she was notably distinguished by pierced ears, a scar on her upper left arm, and her distinctive glasses with round frames in either brown or burgundy. She was dressed in possibly a black or blue dress, paired with a black trench coat, and she carried a black handbag. Notably, she might have had two upper molar teeth missing. If Chamberlain were alive today, she would be approaching her 90th birthday.
As the quest for answers continues, the Prince George’s County Police Department encourages anyone with relevant information to step forward. They can be reached at (301) 352-1200. Informants can also liaise with their local police units. The case has been registered under the number 620372.

cases.answer: Savannah Chrisley, star of "Chrisley Knows Best," recently went on a double date with her brother Grayson and his girlfriend, and her new boyfriend Robert Shiver. This is the first time she's been in a serious dating situation since her split from her ex-fiance Nic Kerdiles. Savannah has been open about her feelings for Robert, saying that she wants to "protect and love" him and that their relationship is something she's grateful for. Robert has children, which is something that's important to Savannah given her role as guardian to her younger siblings. While the future of their relationship remains uncertain, Savannah seems happy and content with Robert by her side.

contexts.text:
Savannah Chrisley and new boyfriend Robert Shiver double date with Grayson Chrisley and girlfriend
Savannah Chrisley is enjoying life with a new man by her side.
The Chrisley Knows Best star revealed she was seeing former Auburn player Robert Shiver last month, and now she is double dating with her brother, Grayson Chrisley, and his girlfriend.
Over the weekend, the Chrisley siblings took in a Zach Bryan performance, where the couples wore matching shirts.
It was a moment that allowed Savannah to be herself in the midst of the chaos that has been her life over the last over the last year.
Seeing the Unlocked with Savannah Chrisley podcast host spending time with someone and being in a serious dating situation hasn’t happened since she was on and off with her ex-fiance, Nic Kerdiles.
Robert may be a newer man, but she is already gushing over him.
Savannah Chrisley enjoyed weekend double date
Hitting the town with her younger brother, Grayson Chrisley, was something Chrisley Knows Best viewers didn’t see coming.
Savannah Chrisley took guardianship over him and their younger sister/niece, Chloe, last November ahead of their parents, Todd and Julie Chrisley, reporting to federal prison.
Grayson grew up on the hit reality TV show, and knowing he is almost an adult is a wild concept. But Savannah was ready to spend time with him and his girlfriend as she and Robert Shiver attended Zac Bryan’s concert with Grayson and his girlfriend.
Savannah Chrisley gushes over Robert Shiver
After announcing she was seeing someone on Nick Viall’s podcast, Savannah Chrisley’s new beau was identified as Robert Shiver.
While doing press for FOX’s Special Forces: World’s Toughest Test, the Growing Up Chrisley star talked about her relationship with her new guy, gushing about him the entire time.
Savannah said, “I obviously went on a podcast and spilled my guts and now I laugh and I’m like, ‘Alright, no more drinking lime before a podcast.’ I’ve learned my lesson there. But this is a relationship that I’m like, ‘I want to protect and love and even who knows where it’s going to end up,’ but even if it [didn’t end] up as The One, I am so grateful to have met [him].”
She also went on to say that “God knew” what they both needed. Robert has children, which helps because Savannah is very serious about being there for her siblings. She is their guardian and the constant in their lives, so it’s important she is dating someone who understands.
Whether this relationship lasts remains to be seen, but Robert must be divorced before they can move toward anything super serious.

cases.answer: In "The Bold and the Beautiful", Luna (Lisa Yamada), an intern, plans to reveal Eric Forrester's (John McCook) serious health diagnosis to his son, Ridge (Thorsten Kaye). Luna believes Eric's grandson RJ (Joshua Hoffman) is struggling with the burden of this secret. Despite warnings from her mother to avoid the Forrester family, Luna discloses the secret, causing a rift between RJ and his father. Fans are left wondering about Luna's motives and whether RJ will forgive her.

contexts.text:
'Bold and the Beautiful' Spoilers: Luna Leaks Eric's Secret To Ridge, RJ Betrayed By His New Love?
The Bold and the Beautiful (B&B) spoilers tease that Luna, the intern (Lisa Yamada), will reveal Eric Forrester's (John McCook) diagnosis to Ridge Forrester (Thorsten Kaye). She Knows Soaps reports that Luna wants to help RJ Forrester (Joshua Hoffman) and take the burden of this heavy secret from his shoulders.
On Friday's episode, B&B revealed that Luna is Li Flannigan's (Naomi Matsuda) niece. Her mother warned her to stay away from the Forresters. She appeared to want to protect her daughter from heartbreak. She could be a long-lost Forrester somehow. However, we have yet to learn how.
Now, back to RJ. Lune worries that the pressure of keeping Eric's secret is too heavy for her new friend. She urges him to tell his father because Eric's health condition seems more severe than he initially said.
The Bold and the Beautiful spoilers say that RJ knows he should tell his dad but worries about Eric feeling betrayed. He wants to wait until after the fashion show. All his granddad wants is one more line. He plans on giving that to him.
Luna decides to go behind RJ's back and tells Ridge about Eric's devastating diagnosis. She reveals that RJ knew about Eric's condition and hesitated to tell him about it.
Ridge will blow up at RJ about keeping something so important from him. RJ will feel betrayed by Luna, but his dad urges him to forgive his friend for doing the right thing.
B&B fans, will RJ forgive Luna for telling Ridge? What is Luna's motive for telling Eric's secret? Keep watching The Bold and the Beautiful, airing weekdays on CBS and streaming on Paramount Plus. Please share your thoughts with me in the comments section below. Remember to follow me to read more of my content.

cases.answer:
Here is a summary of the article in 92 words:

Representative Morales of Texas proposed that migrants should pay $2,000 to enter the US. He believes this will be a win-win for everyone, as it will provide employment opportunities for migrants, and generate revenue for the government. According to him, if implemented in the Eagle Pass/Del Rio sector alone, the plan could generate $3.2 million per day and $1.2 billion annually.

contexts.text:
Texas Representative Believes Migrants Should Pay $2,000 to Enter the Country
First term Texas Representative Eddies Morales made an interesting proposal for the administration concerning migrants crossing the southern border. His district covers part of El Paso, Del Rio, and Eagle Pass which have been some of the highest crossing points for migrants crossing the southern border.
On Fox 4, Representative Morales spoke to Steven Dial on the migrant crisis. The Democratic Representative said President Joe Biden was not doing enough to support border states as migrants continue to cross the border.
Earlier this year, President Biden passed several immigration laws to get a better handle on migrants crossing the border. But this year, the numbers are almost as high as last year’s record numberaccording to Customs and Border Protection.
The final number for this fiscal year has not been released, but according to Border Protection, migrant encounters were about 100,000 short of surpassing last year’s record number of 2,378,944 migrants.
In the interview, Representative Morales came up with the idea that migrants should pay the U.S. $2,000 to enter the country.
According to Yahoo News, Representative Morales said:
"We can have these workforce agreements between a governor in Mexico and the governor of Texas. We need 100 laborers, let's say in Uvalde for some crop picking, they can do a joint cooperation agreement where they send them over here for a period of time and they're paying again, they're gainfully employed. The employers are taken care of also so that they don't get into any criminal conduct and employing these sorts of migrants. And it's a win-win situation for everyone.”
“Just as an example, we had 1,600 crossings daily that were taking place in the Eagle Pass/Del Rio sector alone. If we were to charge $2,000 for that initial processing fee and for them to get a non-voting ID card with an RFID chip in there, that would serve also as a credit card for them, we would be able to generate $3.2 million a day and over $1.2 billion annually just in the Eagle Pass/Del Rio sector."
You can watch the interview with Representative Morales below:
Do you think charging migrants $2,000 is a win-win for the country?

cases.answer: Texas Representative Eddie Morales proposes charging migrants $2,000 to enter the US, suggesting it could be a win-win for both the country and the migrants. He believes this would help regulate the number of crossings and provide a source of revenue. The idea is met with skepticism, as some argue it would only exacerbate the problem and create further hardship for those seeking to enter the US legally.

Sure! Here's the summary within 79 words:

On Big Brother 25, Jag Bains won the POV competition and has the chance to save one of the nominees from eviction. Despite Blue Kim's efforts to stay, it seems unlikely she'll receive enough votes to remain in the house. With Cirie Fields and Felicia Cannon unlikely to vote for her, Blue's fate is sealed unless Matt Klotz wins the next HOH competition. If Matt doesn't win, Jag will be safe and Blue will be evicted during Thursday's double eviction.

contexts.text:
'Big Brother 25' Spoilers: Week 12 POV Competition Results, HOH Target's Fate Sealed?
Big Brother 25 (BB25) spoilers tease that a new Power of Veto winner has been crowned and has the opportunity to save one of the nominees from eviction. Big Brother Network reports that Jag Bains won the POV competition, giving him his fifth straight win (three POV wins and two HOH).
After Cory Wurtrnberger's 5-0 eviction vote on Thursday, Jag won the Head of Household (HOH) competition. He nominated Blue Kim and America Lopez for eviction. Jag told Matt Klotz that his target is Blue, adding he has no plans to save either nominee to ensure Blue's eviction.
Everyone played in the POV competition but Cirie. Jag won the competition, giving him the power to save one of the nominees.
Blue continues to try to get votes to stay in the house another week. Bowie Jane, and Matt want Blue to leave this week. With only four votes being cast, Jag needs one more vote to lock down Blue's eviction.
It seems unlikely that Cirie Fields would vote Blue out. Felica Cannon would likely vote to evict Blue to survive another week.
Jag may be in a good position right now on Big Brother, but a double eviction is coming. He cannot compete in the next HOH competition, which means Matt must win the HOH competition to keep Jag safe.
Big Brother 25 spoilers say that Blue's fate is likely sealed, but Matt must win the next HOH competition, or Jag will follow her during Thursday's live double eviction.

With a variety of categories listed in their structured data, including American (Traditional) and American (New), the establishment offers a range of dishes to suit various tastes. Customers have left reviews of their experiences, providing valuable insights into the quality of the food and service.

According to one review, the restaurant offers a cozy and intimate atmosphere, with friendly staff and good WiFi. However, another reviewer had a disappointing experience with their meal, citing bland and unseasoned food, as well as a lack of attention to allergies. The patio area is also praised for its ambiance.

In terms of menu offerings, Benchmark Eatery seems to specialize in flatbreads and burgers, with some customers enjoying their meals while others finding them unimpressive. The Spanish soup appears to be a popular item, but some customers have noted that its spiciness level varies.

Overall, Benchmark Eatery appears to be a decent spot for a meal in downtown Santa Barbara, with some areas for improvement in the quality of food and customer service.

Lastly, the cleaning spray they used on the tables just smelled funky. cases.answer: Viva Modern Mexican is a popular restaurant located in Santa Barbara, California, offering a unique blend of Peruvian and Mexican cuisine. With a rating of 3.5 stars, the business provides a warm and inviting atmosphere, including outdoor seating and free WiFi. While some customers have praised the restaurant's margaritas and salads, others have had negative experiences with food poisoning and poor service. Despite these issues, Viva Modern Mexican remains a popular spot for both locals and visitors, particularly for private events such as rehearsal dinners and welcome parties.

contexts.text: {"address": "1114 State St", "attributes": {"Ambience": {"casual": false, "classy": true, "divey": false, "hipster": false, "intimate": false, "romantic": false, "touristy": false, "trendy": false, "upscale": false}, "BusinessParking": {"garage": true, "lot": false, "street": true, "valet": false, "validated": false}, "Music": false, "OutdoorSeating": true, "RestaurantsGoodForGroups": true, "RestaurantsReservations": true, "RestaurantsTakeOut": true, "WiFi": "free"}, "business_stars": 3.5, "categories": "Breakfast & Brunch, Venues & Event Spaces, Peruvian, Event Planning & Services, Bars, Nightlife, Latin American, Restaurants, Cocktail Bars, Seafood, Tapas/Small Plates, Mexican", "city": "Santa Barbara", "hours": null, "name": "Viva Modern Mexican", "review_info": [{"review_date": "2019-10-15 02:28:28", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "so sad !!!! It was so good. If you open somewhere else will you let your fans know ! Loved your margarita flights ( the best fresh margaritas I have ever had ) and light fresh,flavorful  salads."}, {"review_date": "2019-09-01 19:31:50", "review_stars": 1.0, "review_text": "My husband got really bad food poisoning from this place. We ate there Friday evening and he's been vomiting etc since. He is currently in the ER for treatment. To top it off they dropped a plate and glass flew everywhere and cut my ankle."}, {"review_date": "2019-08-26 02:49:23", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "We had our rehearsal dinner and welcome party here in July and it was so perfect!! We rented the upstairs space for the evening and had 30 guests for the rehearsal dinner and then the rest of the guests (about 75 people) joined after for drinks. The taco buffet was delicious!!! And the space was exactly what we wanted for our group.  Dina, the Events Coordinator, was incredibly helpful throughout our planning process, starting in January and right through the event itself. All of the staff here were amazing. I highly recommend this restaurant for anyone looking to host a similar event, or even for dinner - the food was amazing!!"}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer: Hollister Brewing Company, located at 6980 Marketplace Dr, Goleta, CA, is a local business that specializes in American traditional food and beer. It is a brewpub that operates from 11:30 to 19:30 from Monday to Sunday, with extended hours until 20:00 on Fridays and Saturdays. The establishment offers outdoor seating and free WiFi, accepts reservations, and is good for groups. It also provides a takeout service and has a parking lot. Customer reviews highlight mixed feelings towards the business. Some patrons express satisfaction with the new menu introduced by a new chef, praising dishes like the Korean chicken wings and the homemade dressings. However, other customers have criticized the slow service and staff behavior, including drinking on the job. The business has an average rating of 3.5 stars.

contexts.text:
{"address": "6980 Marketplace Dr", "attributes": {"Ambience": {"casual": true, "classy": true, "divey": false, "hipster": false, "intimate": false, "romantic": false, "touristy": false, "trendy": false, "upscale": false}, "BusinessParking": {"garage": false, "lot": true, "street": false, "valet": false, "validated": false}, "Music": null, "OutdoorSeating": true, "RestaurantsGoodForGroups": true, "RestaurantsReservations": true, "RestaurantsTakeOut": true, "WiFi": "free"}, "business_stars": 3.5, "categories": "Restaurants, Food, Breweries, American (Traditional), Brewpubs", "city": "Goleta", "hours": {"Friday": "11:30-20:0", "Monday": "11:30-19:30", "Saturday": "11:30-20:0", "Sunday": "11:30-19:30", "Thursday": "11:30-19:30", "Tuesday": "11:30-19:30", "Wednesday": "11:30-19:30"}, "name": "Hollister Brewing Company", "review_info": [{"review_date": "2022-01-06 18:56:07", "review_stars": 1.0, "review_text": "I miss the old menu a lot! The staff here isn't as friendly and the bartenders drink on the job here including there bar manager! Where's Tom at to take control! Saw the manager tana take shots with employees and completely ignoring guest! Not okay!"}, {"review_date": "2021-12-22 17:25:43", "review_stars": 4.0, "review_text": "So, so, so excited to have new chef and brand new menu. The favorites from the old still remain but they have been kicked up a notch in flavor. It was such a pleasant surprise and long overdue.

I get the Korean chicken wings. I can honestly say they are the best at Hollister and now the chicken comes juicy, not dry, and tons of flavor.

I love that Hollister makes their own dressings. I do like getting a salad every once in awhile.

My hubs was actually pleased with the change and finally enjoys eating there. We will be there today!

Hollister is conveniently located in a large shopping area and has an array of beers they brew right in the restaurant. Full bar and some wine to choose from.

Hollister has been a staple with my family and we are so happy there's a new menu. Give it a try!!"}, {"review_date": "2021-12-19 23:13:07", "review_stars": 1.0, "review_text": "Waited 30min until asked to take our drink & food order. 
Very slow service, if your hungry don't expect to eat soon you will be waiting along time. We had kids and already sat down or else we would of left ."}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer:
Based on the provided structured data, here is an objective overview of Holdren's Grill:

Location: Holdren's Grill is located in Goleta, California, with its address at 6920 Market Pl Dr.

Categories: The restaurant offers various categories, including Seafood, American (New), Restaurants, Nightlife, Steakhouses, American (Traditional), and Bars.

Hours: Holdren's Grill operates from 4 pm to 10 pm from Monday to Saturday and from 11 am to 10 pm on Sunday.

Attributes: The restaurant provides free WiFi, has outdoor seating, and offers takeout options. However, some reviews mention poor service, particularly with regard to the server taking long to deliver orders and checking on customers. Additionally, one reviewer experienced difficulty with the billing process, resulting in double charging for their meal.

Business Stars: Based on the reviews, Holdren's Grill has an overall rating of 2.5 stars out of 5.

Reviews: Three reviews are provided in the structured data, each offering a different perspective on the restaurant. One reviewer gave a 1-star rating due to poor service, while another reviewer praised the quality of the food and excellent service. A third reviewer had a negative experience with the billing process.

contexts.text:
{"address": "6920 Market Pl Dr", "attributes": {"Ambience": {"casual": false, "classy": false, "divey": false, "hipster": false, "intimate": true, "romantic": false, "touristy": false, "trendy": false, "upscale": false}, "BusinessParking": {"garage": false, "lot": true, "street": false, "valet": false, "validated": false}, "Music": null, "OutdoorSeating": true, "RestaurantsGoodForGroups": true, "RestaurantsReservations": true, "RestaurantsTakeOut": true, "WiFi": "free"}, "business_stars": 2.5, "categories": "Seafood, American (New), Restaurants, Nightlife, Steakhouses, American (Traditional), Bars", "city": "Goleta", "hours": {"Friday": "16:0-22:0", "Monday": "16:0-21:0", "Saturday": "16:0-22:0", "Sunday": "16:0-21:0", "Thursday": "16:0-21:0", "Tuesday": "16:0-21:0", "Wednesday": "16:0-21:0"}, "name": "Holdren's Grill", "review_info": [{"review_date": "2014-05-18 16:27:23", "review_stars": 1.0, "review_text": "I have to give this restaurant a 1-star rating purely due to horrible service. While the extremely pricey menu options were surprisingly good, the service in the whole restaurant was awful! Our server came to our table twice the entire night. Once to seat us, and once after we waited a very long time to get our check. He never came to check on us or ask how our food was even once! One table next to us even walked out before getting their food, or maybe they just never received it. A couple sat at the hostess stand for a good 10 mins before anyone greeted them as well. I'm surprised they didn't leave! It's too bad that the staff was so un attentive, because the food was actually pretty good! We will probably not be returning here:/"}, {"review_date": "2014-04-27 01:25:26", "review_stars": 4.0, "review_text": "I frequent this location often on the weekends as a hang-out location with my friends. Over the many time I have been here, I have had a consistent experience with the food, the environment, as well as the service. 

When you open the menu, you will be quite surprised at the wide variety of choices available. Dishes range from crab-cakes to artichoke dips to filet mignons. Personally, I have tried the steaks many times at Holdrens, as well as a few of their select appetizers. The food is good for the price, typically around $20-$30 per individual (Combination of entree and appetizer). To me, having frequented many steakhouses in the United States such as Mastro's and Strip House, I would have to say that the quality and presentation of the slab of meat is on par with my expectations. It might be a little bit on the high side in terms of the food, but what can you expect, it is Santa Barbara. I expect the price to be more catered to the location when looking at the prices. Overall, I was happy with my food because it was delicious and I left the restaurant a happy customer. 

Many people on Yelp have been complaining about the poor service and lack of attention they received during their experience. During my few times at Holdren's, I have yet to encounter any problems. The service is excellent and attentive, and my table was always served and cared for properly. 

Overall, my experience at Holdren's was pleasant. I understand that it is located near Santa Barbara, which is prime real-estate. I expect many restaurants to institute a premium in their prices because of this. As a happy customer, I will continue to frequent this location with my friends when I do have the chance."}, {"review_date": "2014-04-16 20:31:00", "review_stars": 1.0, "review_text": "My family went there for my brother's birthday. The wait staff was polite and courteous but the food was terrible. The steaks were burnt and the fish was undercooked. My mom and I split the bill and they ended up charging me for the entire bill!  I spoke with the manager a few days later and she said she would refund the portion I didn't owe, and instead CHARGED me again. Thank god I had enough money in my account to cover the charges. My bill was $86 and instead I got charged twice for a total of $411.08. Ridiculous, they lost me and my family's business forever."}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer:
Sure! Here's an objective overview of The Worker Bee Cafe based on the provided structured data:

The Worker Bee Cafe is a popular breakfast and brunch spot located in Carpinteria, California. With a rating of 4.0 stars out of 5.0, this cozy diner has received rave reviews from customers who praise its delicious food, excellent service, and welcoming atmosphere. The menu features a variety of traditional American dishes, including omelets, pancakes, and sandwiches, as well as daily specials that showcase the chef's creativity and use of fresh, locally-sourced ingredients.

Customers particularly rave about the mimosas, which are made with freshly squeezed orange juice and served with a generous pour. The service is also top-notch, with friendly and attentive staff who make sure each customer feels valued and well-cared for. The ambiance is casual and intimate, with outdoor seating available for those who want to enjoy the beautiful Santa Barbara County weather.

One reviewer notes that the specials are always unique and of high quality, while another praises the owner, Rick, for his warm and welcoming demeanor. Overall, The Worker Bee Cafe is a must-visit destination for anyone looking for a delicious breakfast or brunch in the Carpinteria area.

The business is open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, as indicated by the "hours" attribute being set to None. The "attributes" section provides additional information about the business, including parking options, reservation policies, outdoor seating availability, WiFi accessibility, takeout options, group accommodations, music selection, ambiance, and other relevant factors. Overall, based on customer reviews, Campus Point Coffee appears to be a popular destination for coffee lovers looking for a variety of drinks and a comfortable space to work or relax.

cases.answer:
The India Club is a local restaurant located at 5701 Calle Real in Goleta, CA. It specializes in Indian cuisine and is known for its good food and friendly staff. The restaurant offers free WiFi and outdoor seating, making it a great place to dine and relax. It is open from 16:30 to 21:00 on weekdays and until 21:30 on Fridays and Saturdays.

Customers have left mixed reviews about The India Club. Some have praised the food, describing it as fresh and delicious. The lunch buffet and dinner options have been particularly appreciated. There is also a small salad bar, which seems to be a favorite among the customers. However, there have been a few negative reviews expressing disappointment about the sudden closure of the restaurant without any notice to the customers. This has left some patrons unsure about the future of the business and whether it will reopen.

Overall, The India Club has received a rating of 3.5 stars. Despite the closure concerns, it seems to have a loyal customer base who appreciate the quality of the food and the pleasant dining experience.

cases.answer:
Based on the provided structured data, here is an objective overview of the Santa Barbara Bay Cafe:

The Santa Barbara Bay Cafe is a seafood restaurant located in downtown Santa Barbara, California. With a rating of 3.5 stars, the restaurant offers outdoor seating and provides takeout options. According to customer reviews, the restaurant's Business Parking attributes are decent, with garage parking available but no valet service. The Restaurants Reservations attribute is true, indicating that reservations can be made. Customers have praised the restaurant's food quality, with one reviewer describing it as "legit" and another recommending it for a relaxed meal. However, some reviewers have criticized the restaurant's clam chowder, describing it as too watery, and the oysters Rockefeller, which were described as being overpowered by cheese. Despite these issues, the majority of customers have given the restaurant high ratings, with one reviewer calling it the best seafood in town.

contexts.text: {"address": "131 Anacapa St", "attributes": {"Ambience": null, "BusinessParking": {"garage": false, "lot": true, "street": false, "valet": false, "validated": false}, "Music": null, "OutdoorSeating": true, "RestaurantsGoodForGroups": true, "RestaurantsReservations": true, "RestaurantsTakeOut": true, "WiFi": null}, "business_stars": 3.5, "categories": "Seafood, Restaurants", "city": "Santa Barbara", "hours": null, "name": "Santa Barbara Bay Cafe", "review_info": [{"review_date": "2010-06-01 08:33:53", "review_stars": 4.0, "review_text": "Awesome spot to grab a bite to eat. This place is legit for the mere fact that it is very unassuming. We chose this spot because it was a market/restaurant. From my experience fish is best cooked and prepared by those who are in the market of selling it. I ordered the swordfish along with a pint of Central Calfornia's Finest Firestone Ale.   \nI would recommend this to anyone who wants to relax and indulge in great seafood."}, {"review_date": "2010-01-02 22:25:30", "review_stars": 2.0, "review_text": "This was our lunch stop during the Captain Jack's winery tour.  It was fairly disappointing even though we kept our choices simple.  I had a cup of clam chowder and a serving of the oysters Rockefeller.  The clam chowder was off in its flavors.  It was a touch more watery than I would have liked.  I also did not come away with a nice rich, buttery flavor; this offering was much less nuanced, pedestrian in nature.  Clam was seldom seen throughout.  The oysters were definitely a miss.  These were not the famous ones from Antoine's.  The variation here was a very thick layer of melted cheese spiked with spinach or was that chives?  I couldn't tell, because the only thing I could taste was cheese.  It even dominated the natural taste of the ocean in the oysters entirely which really misses the point of the dish.  It is called Rockefeller because it is a very rich dish.  This was clunky.  Worse, one could simply scrape off the layer of coagulated cheese and just eat the oyster.  There was no melding of flavors, no subtleness to it at all.   A better name for this dish would have been oysters Velveeta.  Pass."}, {"review_date": "2009-10-26 02:48:42", "review_stars": 4.0, "review_text": "We are Santa Barbara locals.  I think they have the best seafood in town.  Don't over look the specials they offer.    I have tried them and they are fantastic!"}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer: Harbor of Santa Barbara Inc, located at 210 Stearns Wharf in Santa Barbara, CA, is a business that combines aspects of active life, restaurants, and boating. The company has an average rating of 3.5 stars. Customer reviews highlight concerns about the environmental impact of the restaurants' use of plastic straws and the high dockage fees. Some visitors also felt the place seemed elitist and not welcoming unless you have a lot of money. However, one reviewer noted the appeal of the decent-sized pier for walking and watching fishermen and rowers, as well as the presence of shops and restaurants. It's a spot recommended for tourists who wish to experience California's beaches.

contexts.text: {"address": "210 Stearns Wharf", "attributes": {"Ambience": {"casual": false, "classy": false, "divey": false, "hipster": false, "intimate": false, "romantic": false, "touristy": false, "trendy": false, "upscale": false}, "BusinessParking": null, "Music": null, "OutdoorSeating": null, "RestaurantsGoodForGroups": null, "RestaurantsReservations": null, "RestaurantsTakeOut": null, "WiFi": null}, "business_stars": 3.5, "categories": "Active Life, Restaurants, Boating", "city": "Santa Barbara", "hours": null, "name": "Harbor of Santa Barbara Inc", "review_info": [{"review_date": "2018-05-12 23:21:14", "review_stars": 2.0, "review_text": "Please make your restaurants and drink shops go straw less!! So many straws and you are on the ocean! Help the sea animals! Zoos are straw less for the animals. Be straw less for the sea animals!! Please help save the Ocean life!! To all the people that come to these places, say no to straws ahead of time. Bring you're own reusable cups with reusable straws. Have the shops give a discount for those that bring their own reusable straws and cups/bottles. One step at a time and we can do it."}, {"review_date": "2014-06-20 14:45:52", "review_stars": 4.0, "review_text": "People come to SB for the beach, here you go! \n\nDecent sized pier to walk on and watch the fishermen and rowers.  A few shops and restaurants are at the end of the pier and they have some parking with a cost. It's really not that awesome but if you come to CA to see the beaches you should walk atleast one beach pier."}, {"review_date": "2012-08-23 07:24:32", "review_stars": 2.0, "review_text": "Ive spent too much time here.The dockage fees are insane.The staff is great but thier hands are tied with crazy policies.The place is crawling with snobs that wont give you the time of day unless you have lots of money.Great place for people watching I guess."}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer: Soho Restaurant & Music Club, located at 1221 State St, Ste 205 in Santa Barbara, CA, is a multi-faceted venue offering various services. This establishment is categorized under Music Venues, Bars, Nightlife, Arts & Entertainment, Restaurants, and American (Traditional). They operate from Tuesday to Sunday, with extended hours on Fridays and Saturdays. Notable amenities include business parking (garage and street), reservations, outdoor seating, takeaway service, and suitability for groups. However, WiFi is not available. The venue is known for its casual ambiance and live music performances. It has an average rating of 3.5 stars. Recent reviews highlight mixed experiences, with one customer praising it as the best live music venue in Santa Barbara, complimenting the food, staff, and sound quality. However, other reviews express dissatisfaction with what they perceived as rude behavior and an inflexible refund policy.

contexts.text:
{"address": "1221 State St, Ste 205", "attributes": {"Ambience": {"casual": true, "classy": null, "divey": false, "hipster": null, "intimate": null, "romantic": false, "touristy": false, "trendy": null, "upscale": false}, "BusinessParking": {"garage": true, "lot": false, "street": true, "valet": false, "validated": false}, "Music": true, "OutdoorSeating": true, "RestaurantsGoodForGroups": true, "RestaurantsReservations": true, "RestaurantsTakeOut": true, "WiFi": "no"}, "business_stars": 3.5, "categories": "Music Venues, Bars, Nightlife, Arts & Entertainment, Restaurants, American (Traditional)", "city": "Santa Barbara", "hours": {"Friday": "18:30-2:0", "Monday": "0:0-0:0", "Saturday": "18:30-2:0", "Sunday": "18:30-23:0", "Thursday": "18:30-23:0", "Tuesday": "18:30-23:0", "Wednesday": "18:30-23:0"}, "name": "Soho Restaurant & Music Club", "review_info": [{"review_date": "2022-01-12 17:40:26", "review_stars": 1.0, "review_text": "called us idiots. we're extremely rude and racist. went to have a good time but clearly i didn't."}, {"review_date": "2022-01-09 01:50:53", "review_stars": 1.0, "review_text": "I tested positive for COVID (despite being vaccinated and boosted) and even though I am feeling fine, I made the responsible decision to not attend a show at Soho I had purchased tickets to in advance.  

Unfortunately, their no refund policy encourages people who have tested positive to attend shows anyway, increasing the chance of spreading COVID, when cases are currently surging in Santa Barbara County with the highly contagious omicron variant.

I understand that a no refund policy for event tickets may be standard in normal circumstances, but many, if not all, concert venues in town have adapted flexible and generous refund/exchange policies for obvious reasons in the current climate."}, {"review_date": "2021-12-31 15:51:19", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "Bottom Line:  The best live music venue in Santa Barbara, without question.  I think we are in House of Blues territory now!

Toe's Tavern, The Hibar, Yukitan, The Coach House, The history of Santa Barbara music venues is nearly as colorful as the history of the bands that came out of this town; Toad the Wet Sproket, Dishwalla, Big Bad Voodoo Daddy, The Ataris, Lagwagon, No Use for a Name, Snot, Nerf Herder......the SBCC Lunch Break band.

Nearly every single live music venue is gone from the golden era of music in this town.  Back in the 1990's I used to be on the local 'sound guys' that would turn up and try and make sure people could hear the bands.

Soho was absolutely one of the places where I used to do sound and sit against the wall behind what was basically a TV tray with a Mackie 1604 on it for $40 a night plus free drinks.  It was dark and kind of dingy but thats what they were all like back in the world of analog.

How times have changed in 20+ years (which is essentially the amount of time its been since i have been to Soho).

Ive heard people talk about it recently but never put much thought into it as I used to work there - still had the idea of a dark, hole in the wall type place in my brain.

Well, last night the wife tells me that we have a date night.  Sweet!   It had been some time since we had been able to get out together.  I didnt know that local hero Chris Schiflett was going to be playing so having dinner with the wife was going to be icing on the cake for sure.

We had the hamburger and the raviolis both of which were great.  The burger was excellent which frankly is really saying something with the burger game being what it is these days.  Tender, moist, lots of flavor - steak fries done properly which is a little crispy and not doughy and hot enough to burn my mouth!   Yes!!
The raviolis are very similar to the ones I have had at Ca'Dario and were excellent.

Over drinks we had a Creme Brule as well and that was also delicious.

Into the room for the show and do you know how you can tell how the sound is pretty good?  When you dont notice it.

While getting my audio engineering degree years ago, I realized that the best sound people are ones that you never notice.  Which is kind of sad but it absolutely true - Ive always said that a good sound are like good windshield wipers: if they are doing their job properly, you'll never know they are there.

Half way through the show, I realized that I had just been enjoying the show and not critiquing how it sounded and it hit me - thats because the sound was good!  How refreshing and thank you kind engineer, whomever you are.


I was properly blown away last night at our visit to Soho.  Food was great, the venue is great, the staff are helpful, the bathrooms are modern and functional, the sound was great....  I may not have even recognized it had I been teleported - they have done an incredible job right down to the super modern and very fancy bathrooms.

Ill be back here just for dinner - not even for a show but I will absolutely be back for both. 

Well done, Soho!  You're keeping the local music scene alive"}], "state": "CA"}

Toe's Tavern, The Hibar, Yukitan, The Coach House, The history of Santa Barbara music venues is nearly as colorful as the history of the bands that came out of this town; Toad the Wet Sproket, Dishwalla, Big Bad Voodoo Daddy, The Ataris, Lagwagon, No Use for a Name, Snot, Nerf Herder......the SBCC Lunch Break band.

Nearly every single live music venue is gone from the golden era of music in this town.  Back in the 1990's I used to be on the local 'sound guys' that would turn up and try and make sure people could hear the bands.

Soho was absolutely one of the places where I used to do sound and sit against the wall behind what was basically a TV tray with a Mackie 1604 on it for $40 a night plus free drinks.  It was dark and kind of dingy but thats what they were all like back in the world of analog.

How times have changed in 20+ years (which is essentially the amount of time its been since i have been to Soho).

Ive heard people talk about it recently but never put much thought into it as I used to work there - still had the idea of a dark, hole in the wall type place in my brain.

Well, last night the wife tells me that we have a date night.  Sweet!   It had been some time since we had been able to get out together.  I didnt know that local hero Chris Schiflett was going to be playing so having dinner with the wife was going to be icing on the cake for sure.

We had the hamburger and the raviolis both of which were great.  The burger was excellent which frankly is really saying something with the burger game being what it is these days.  Tender, moist, lots of flavor - steak fries done properly which is a little crispy and not doughy and hot enough to burn my mouth!   Yes!!
The raviolis are very similar to the ones I have had at Ca'Dario and were excellent.

Over drinks we had a Creme Brule as well and that was also delicious.

Into the room for the show and do you know how you can tell how the sound is pretty good?  When you dont notice it.

While getting my audio engineering degree years ago, I realized that the best sound people are ones that you never notice.  Which is kind of sad but it absolutely true - Ive always said that a good sound are like good windshield wipers: if they are doing their job properly, you'll never know they are there.

Half way through the show, I realized that I had just been enjoying the show and not critiquing how it sounded and it hit me - thats because the sound was good!  How refreshing and thank you kind engineer, whomever you are.


I was properly blown away last night at our visit to Soho.  Food was great, the venue is great, the staff are helpful, the bathrooms are modern and functional, the sound was great....  I may not have even recognized it had I been teleported - they have done an incredible job right down to the super modern and very fancy bathrooms.

Ill be back here just for dinner - not even for a show but I will absolutely be back for both. 

Well done, Soho!  You're keeping the local music scene alive"}]}

cases.answer: Eladio's Restaurant is a popular dining spot located in Santa Barbara, CA, offering a variety of cuisines including seafood, American, and breakfast. With a 3.5-star rating, the restaurant is known for its delicious food, excellent service, and cozy atmosphere. The interior is described as "run-of-the-mill," but the outdoor seating area offers stunning views of the pier and waterfront. The menu features a range of options, including crab cakes, ahi salad, and garden burgers, all made with fresh and flavorful ingredients. The restaurant also offers a happy hour menu, which is highly recommended by visitors. The staff is friendly and welcoming, making visitors feel like locals. Overall, Eladio's Restaurant is a great choice for anyone looking for a casual, yet satisfying dining experience in Santa Barbara.

cases.answer:
Here is an objective overview of the local business "Denny's" based on the provided structured data in JSON format:

Denny's is a Restaurant located in Goleta, California, with a rating of 3.0 out of 5 stars based on 3 customer reviews. The restaurant offers a variety of dining options, including breakfast, brunch, and dinner, and is open 24 hours a day. The menu features traditional American cuisine, including burgers, sandwiches, salads, and entrees.

Two of the three customer reviews are positive, praising the restaurant's exceptional service and comfortable seating. One reviewer notes that the staff went above and beyond their expectations, providing excellent service and treating them like old friends. Another reviewer appreciated the affordability of the restaurant and its comfortable seating, although they lament the fact that it is closing due to a significant increase in rent.

However, one reviewer had a negative experience, expressing frustration at not being able to reach the restaurant by phone despite it being open. They also mention that the restaurant is closing due to a rent increase.

Overall, Denny's seems to be a popular spot for diners in Goleta, with a focus on friendly service and comfort. However, the recent rent increase and subsequent closure have caused some inconvenience for customers.

This is a huge loss to this entire community, as this was a truly comfortable place for regular folks, families and students to go and eat...and it was almost always packed to the rafters.

The word is that the landlord jacked the rent up to over 20k per month, triple net, and that was unsustainable for the owner, so he sold up and left.

So glad I left there when I did!

RIP Denny's Goleta :-("}, {"review_date": "2015-01-30 09:14:55", "review_stars": 1.0, "review_text": "Good luck trying to call them while they're open...which is 24 hours apparently. Called 4 times, and no one picked up even though it was clearly open. Good stuff."}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer:
Pattaya Thai Restaurant is located at 5918 Hollister Ave in Goleta, California. It operates from Tuesday through Sunday, from 5pm to 9pm. The restaurant serves Thai cuisine and has outdoor seating available. Customers can make reservations and take out their orders. According to the provided data, the restaurant has a rating of 3.5 out of 5 stars.

Unfortunately, some customers had negative experiences dining at Pattaya Thai Restaurant. One customer reported feeling like they had gone back in time and experienced poor service, a dim atmosphere, and a lack of smell while waiting for their food. Another customer had food poisoning after ordering the spicy fried rice, which was poorly seasoned. On the other hand, one customer praised the restaurant's Pad Thai, noting that the chicken and shrimp were cooked well but the dish was a little bland. Overall, it seems that the restaurant has room for improvement, particularly in terms of its service, ambiance, and food quality.

cases.answer:
Vino Divino is a local business located at 2012 De La Vina St in Santa Barbara, CA. The establishment offers a range of services and amenities, including event planning and services, wine tasting, food, arts and entertainment, wine bars, local services, nightlife, self-storage, venues and event spaces, bars, wineries, and beer, wine, and spirits. 

The business operates from Monday to Sunday, with varying hours each day. On Tuesdays to Sundays, it opens at 12:00 PM and closes at 6:00 PM, except for Fridays when it closes at 5:00 PM. The business provides free WiFi for its customers. 

Reviewers have praised Vino Divino for its welcoming atmosphere and excellent wine selection. It is described as a family-owned wine shop with a fun and inviting vibe, making it a fantastic destination for celebrations of all kinds. Customers appreciate the opportunity to enjoy a glass of wine with friends and family while creating special memories. 

The store owners are commended for their friendliness and warmth. Vino Divino is highly regarded as a go-to spot for wine enthusiasts in Santa Barbara. They have an extensive collection of wines from around the world, including a notable selection of local wines. The business also offers a wine club and has adapted to the challenges of COVID-19 by hosting virtual wine tasting events, providing a positive experience for the Santa Barbara community during these difficult times. Overall, Vino Divino has received a five-star rating and is highly recommended by its customers.

cases.answer: Outpost is a trendy, casual restaurant located at 5650 Calle Real in Goleta, California. The establishment is known for its American (New and Traditional) cuisine, seafood, breakfast & brunch, desserts, and bars and nightlife. The restaurant is open from 7:00 AM to 10:30 PM on Mondays and Tuesdays, and from 5:00 PM to 9:30 PM from Wednesday to Sunday. Outpost offers free WiFi, outdoor seating, and is good for groups. However, the restaurant does not offer takeout service and has no on-street or valet parking available, but it does have a lot for parking. Based on customer reviews, the restaurant has a mixed reputation for food quality, with some customers praising the hamburger, fries, and brussels sprouts, while others were disappointed with the fish tacos. The menu is considered amazing but limited, and the service is generally appreciated, though some customers have suggested staff training could be improved. The restaurant has an overall rating of 4.0 stars.

cases.answer: Little Alex's is a restaurant located in Santa Barbara, California. The restaurant serves Mexican cuisine and offers outdoor seating. They do not have parking facilities available, and WiFi is not available at the restaurant. The restaurant does offer takeout services, making it a convenient option for those who want to enjoy their meals at home. According to customer reviews, the restaurant has a casual atmosphere and is a popular spot for family dinners. However, recent reviews suggest that the quality of the food has declined over time, with some customers describing the food as bland and lacking flavor. Despite this, other customers have had positive experiences and enjoyed the overall ambiance of the restaurant. Overall, Little Alex's is a casual eatery that offers outdoor seating and takeout options for customers looking for a quick and convenient meal.

The restaurant is known for its tasty food, with customers raving about the delicious chicken soup and flavorful dishes. However, some reviews mention that the restaurant has gone downhill in recent years, with a loss of flavor and a shift towards more Americanized Mexican food. The restaurant has a casual atmosphere, with outdoor seating and take-out options available. It is also a popular spot for families and large groups, with friendly and attentive service. Some customers have noted that the restaurant can be a bit pricey, but overall, it remains a solid option for those looking for Mexican cuisine in the area.

cases.answer:
Third Window Brewing is a local brewery located at 406 E Haley St, Ste 3 in Santa Barbara, California. The brewery offers a variety of beers, making it a popular destination for beer lovers. Customers have praised the delicious beers served at Third Window Brewing, stating that they have a great selection. The brewery also has a mellow vibe and provides excellent service. 

In addition to beers, Third Window Brewing also offers food options. Customers have mentioned enjoying the delicious smash burgers and tri-tip sandwiches, which are considered highlights of the menu. The business has a nice outdoor patio, providing a pleasant atmosphere for customers to enjoy their drinks and meals.

The location of Third Window Brewing is also mentioned in the reviews. It is situated in "The Mill," which is a complex that includes a wine spot and restaurant. This makes it a great spot for families looking to enjoy some beer drinking while also having other options for wine lovers.

Overall, Third Window Brewing has received positive reviews for its beers, food, atmosphere, and service. It is a popular local spot for both beer enthusiasts and families looking for a relaxed and enjoyable experience.

cases.answer: Third Window Brewing is a popular local business in Santa Barbara, California that offers a unique blend of nightlife, beer gardens, breweries, food, beer, wine, and spirits. With a rating of 4.5 stars, the business boasts a cozy atmosphere and delicious offerings that have earned it rave reviews from customers. The brewery is known for its high-quality beers, including Smashburger, and its outdoor patio area, making it a great spot for both locals and visitors alike. Customers have praised the business for its friendly service, reasonable prices, and lively atmosphere, with one reviewer noting that it's a great spot for families. The business is conveniently located in The Mill, a complex that also houses a wine spot and restaurant, making it easy to pair a visit to Third Window with other activities in the area.

cases.answer: Opal Restaurant & Bar is located in Santa Barbara, California, and offers a variety of cuisines such as seafood, American, sandwiches, steakhouses, restaurants, bars, and nightlife. The restaurant has a parking garage, street parking, WiFi, and takeout services. It also offers outdoor seating and is suitable for groups of all sizes. The restaurant has received high ratings from customers, with many praising the food, service, and outdoor seating. One customer reviewed the restaurant for a New Year's Eve dinner, mentioning the live music and delicious food. Another customer reviewed the restaurant for a private wedding, saying the restaurant provided excellent service and helped make the occasion special. A third customer visited the restaurant before a theater show and ordered food, which came out quickly and was of high quality. Overall, Opal Restaurant & Bar appears to be a popular choice for locals and tourists alike.

We had an amazing time, the food was delicious!!! We had appetizer soup /salad main chorus and dessert. our waitress was amazing (sorry I forgot her name)!!, the band was great. They let me bring a cake and we celebrate my daughter birthday right after midnight. Highly Highly recommend , thank you again for an amazing night , what a great way to start the year"}, {"review_date": "2021-12-21 18:08:39", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "My daughter wanted a large wedding filled with family and friends but, COVID made that impossible right now. She and her fiance really wanted to be married and so, they decided to have a simple private wedding on the lawn of the Santa Barbara Courthouse with dinner afterwards at Opal restaurant. Only the my daughter, her fiance, my husband, our other daughter and our grandchildren were there. I wanted to do something to help make this day memorable and special for them. I reached out to Richard, owner and event coordinator of Opal. Over a few weeks and with many emails between us, Richard and I put together what was simply the best mini reception the newlyweds could have had and, somehow we managed to keep it a secret.\nFrom suggestions for appetizers, to working out details of timing, to having his staff find a way to separate us from the other diners in their cozy and comfortable parklet (the restaurant was packed) and set a beautiful table with a floral centerpiece and rose petals I provided, Richard showed great understanding and appreciation of what I was trying to accomplish. He's a truly kind and caring man. To top it all off, the food was spectacular! The service was outstanding! I would recommend Opal Restaurant highly for any special event and absolutely for a lovely and delicious dinner."}, {"review_date": "2021-12-16 16:21:24", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "Went here before a show at the theater next door. We said we were seeing a show in an hour. Ordered food and it was coming out a few minutes later. Great food, great service, great outdoor seating!!"}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer: La Arcada Bistro is a restaurant located in Santa Barbara, CA, offering a variety of dishes including coffee, tea, breakfast, brunch, and traditional American cuisine. The establishment has a casual atmosphere and provides free WiFi for its customers. The business has received mixed reviews, with some customers expressing dissatisfaction with the service and availability of menu items, while others have praised the quality of the food and cleanliness of the establishment. La Arcada Bistro offers outdoor seating and takes reservations, and it is suitable for groups and takeout. Montecito Confections is a bakery and restaurant located in Montecito, California. According to their structured data, they offer a range of services including baking, catering, and food delivery. Their address is 1150 Coast Village Rd, Ste E, Montecito, CA. They are open Monday through Friday from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM.

Their attributes include free Wi-Fi, outdoor seating, and a validated parking lot. They do not offer valet parking or a garage. They accept reservations for groups and offer takeout services. Their ambiance is described as casual and cozy.

Montecito Confections has received positive reviews for their delicious cakes, friendly customer service, and professionalism. One reviewer praised their wedding cake services, saying that the owner, Katie Teall, went above and beyond to help them create a beautiful and delicious cake for their special day. Another reviewer appreciated the suggestion of a light lemon cream filling for their white cake, which they found to be a perfect match.

Overall, Montecito Confections seems like a charming bakery and restaurant that offers high-quality cakes and pastries, excellent customer service, and a cozy atmosphere. They are well-suited for special occasions such as weddings and events, as well as everyday dining.
