When I placed the order they suggested a light lemon cream filling which I went with. It was beyond perfect. Light fluffy buttercream, moist cake, and the filling was a perfect match up. Yep, I will be back!'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2014-08-07 19:34:34', 'review_text': 'Katie baked the wedding cake of my dreams. It came out exactly as I wanted and was delicious. We had three tiers: two were red velvet and one was vanilla custard cake with fresh raspberries. I am very happy with our decision to choose her.'}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2014-05-09 15:00:38', 'review_text': 'Katie Teall is an ANGEL!!!  I made a wedding cake for the daughter of a very close High School friend and the Fondant I bought (FondArt) was horrible.  I spent about 7 hours kneading it and rolling it, and no matter what I did when I covered a tier of the cake it cracked.  I was so stressed that decided to go to Montecito Confections to see if I could buy Fondant there.  I got there and asked Katie Teall the sweet adorable, British owner if she could help me.  she explained that she sold the store and the rest of her Fondant was for the last 15 wedding cakes that she had to deliver during the summer.  She must have seen my face of stress, exhaustion, just lost, and asked:  How big is your cake?  I told her and she said:  Do you want me to cover it in Fondant for you?  I jumped to hug her and kiss her and hug her and kissed her some more.

I came home and the mother of the bride carried the bottom tier and we went back to Montecito Confections to be saved by Katie.  We arrived and she said where is the rest of the cake?  I did not want to take advantage so we just took the biggest tier.  She said:  Bring it!

Home we went after hugging Katie and kissing her some more and got help and left with two more tiers.  Katie my angel covered the three tiers!!! She was my angel.  The mother of the bride broke in tears, I guess she was worried that I was never going to serve the wedding cake.  These were tears of relief!  We ended up having the most beautiful cake and thanks to Katie.  Katie also build the cake, putting all three tiers together.  Then explained to me how to decorate it.  She told me to get a photo of the container of her fondant because she had tried all the different brands and that was the best of them all.  She said that mine was impossible to work with since it was too dry!  Katie is a walking ANGEL.  I love her, love her and love her.  All the kisses and hugs are not enough to thank her for saving the wedding cake and so much more.'}]}

Your Place Thai Restaurant is located in Santa Barbara, California, and offers Thai cuisine. The restaurant is open from Monday to Sunday, with hours ranging from 11:30 AM to 9:30 PM on weekdays and until 10:30 PM on weekends. The restaurant has attributes such as reservation options, outdoor seating, WiFi availability, and takeout options. However, it does not have valet parking or validated parking. 

The restaurant has a rating of 4.0 out of 5 stars and has received several reviews. One customer gave the restaurant a low rating of 1 star, stating that they were charged extra for every little item, including sauces, and that the food was of poor quality. Another customer gave the restaurant a high rating of 5 stars and praised the restaurant's yellow curry, which they said was better than their Penang curry. They also noted that the takeout food was good, and the tofu and chicken were always fresh. A third customer also gave the restaurant a high rating of 5 stars and called it their favorite authentic Thai restaurant in Santa Barbara. They praised the restaurant's soups and Pad Thai and found the prices fair. Overall, the restaurant appears to have mixed reviews, with some customers praising the food and service while others had negative experiences.

Our Daily Bread is a local bakery and restaurant located at 831 Santa Barbara St in Santa Barbara, CA. The business offers a variety of baked goods, gluten-free options, and breakfast & brunch items. The bakery is open from 6:00 am to 5:30 pm from Monday to Friday and from 7:00 am to 4:00 pm on Saturdays.

Customers have left mixed reviews about the business. One customer praised the great service and food they received during their visits. They particularly enjoyed the salads and desserts and recommended the place as a casual lunch spot. On the other hand, another customer expressed disappointment with their experience. They mentioned changes in the sandwich menu and a lack of pastry options. They also complained about receiving the wrong sandwich and the high prices.

The business has a rating of 3.5 stars. The provided structured data does not include information about restaurant reservations, takeout service, WiFi availability, or whether the restaurant is good for groups. However, it does mention that outdoor seating is available.

Overall, Our Daily Bread is a local bakery and restaurant that offers a range of food options. While some customers have had positive experiences, others have expressed dissatisfaction with the menu changes and service.

Based on the provided structured data, here is an objective overview of Aksum Restaurant in Santa Barbara:

Aksum Restaurant is a highly-rated Ethiopian restaurant located in downtown Santa Barbara. Customers have praised the restaurant's friendly staff and delicious food, particularly the vegetable and meat combos which are popular choices among first-time visitors. The restaurant offers indoor seating and takeout options, and has a rating of 5.0 stars. Reviewers have also noted that the restaurant is a great option for groups and that the atmosphere is welcoming. While the restaurant does not offer business parking or reservations, it has Wifi available and allows takeout orders. Overall, Aksum Restaurant has received glowing reviews for its tasty Ethiopian cuisine and excellent customer service.

According to customer reviews, the restaurant offers delicious and authentic Ethiopian cuisine, with standout dishes including the vegetable and meat combos. The staff is known for their friendly and attentive service, making visitors feel welcome and at home. The restaurant has a cozy atmosphere with outdoor seating, and Wi-Fi is available for patrons. While the restaurant does not take reservations, it is a great option for groups and take-out. Customers have given the restaurant a perfect 5-star rating, praising the food, service, and ambiance. Aksum Restaurant is only open for lunch, but it is a must-visit spot for anyone looking for flavorful Ethiopian cuisine in Santa Barbara.

cases.answer: The Tully, located at 1431 San Andres St, Santa Barbara, CA, is a highly-rated local bar known for Karaoke and nightlife. The establishment has a 4.5-star rating and is praised for its friendly service, relaxed atmosphere, and excellent drinks menu. Customers particularly appreciate its extensive happy hour, which runs from Monday to Friday and extends until 7 pm, a rarity in the area. The Tully also offers free WiFi and is a great spot for groups. The venue features outdoor seating and a pool table, adding to its casual vibe. However, it's worth noting that parking options are limited as there are no garage, street, validated, lot, or valet services available. Customers also highlighted their memorable experiences with the educational drink services and festive decor during the holiday season.

cases.answer: El Pastorcito is a Mexican restaurant located in Santa Barbara, California. The business offers a variety of options, including tacos and burritos, and has a rating of 5 stars on Google. The customers who reviewed the business speak highly of the quality of the food, with particular praise for the mulitas de pastor and the horchata drinks. One customer mentions seeing a crowd of people gathered around the taco truck at East Beach on S Milpas Street, and decided to check it out themselves. They were satisfied with their experience and returned for more. Another customer reports having the birria tacos, which they found to be fine, but the el pastor was the standout dish. Overall, it seems that El Pastorcito is a popular spot for Mexican cuisine in Santa Barbara, with a strong focus on authentic flavors and high-quality ingredients.

cases.answer:
Based on the provided structured data, here is an objective overview of Tiburon Tavern:

Tiburon Tavern is a local dive bar located in Santa Barbara, California, offering a cozy atmosphere and a range of amenities. With a rating of 4.0 stars, the bar has gained a positive reputation among customers, who praise its friendly staff, cleanliness, and modern jukebox. While it may not be as lively as other bars in the area, Tiburon Tavern offers a unique experience for those seeking a more relaxed setting. Customers can enjoy a variety of beers, including craft options, and take advantage of the bar's Wi-Fi. The establishment is open seven days a week, with varying hours depending on the day of the week. While parking may be limited, the bar offers street parking and validated parking options. Overall, Tiburon Tavern is a great spot for locals and visitors alike, offering a welcoming atmosphere and quality drinks.

I came in this time around on a Thursday night, fiesta week, and there were surprisingly just a few other patrons. Talk about a hidden gem of a spot. Sandy, our bartender was friendly and efficient and made sure that our group was taken care of. 

The bar was clean and organized. Even the bathroom was tidy which is a rare occurrence for dive bars. I have only ever ordered beers from here but they do have a full selection for your needs. Prior to the other evening, I had only ever come here for their Friday night karaoke events. They have always been quite popular and there's typically a packed room. I feel that the gentleman hosting the event does a wonderful job and the music selection is superior. 

I came in this time around on a Thursday night, fiesta week, and there were surprisingly just a few other patrons. Talk about a hidden gem of a spot. Sandy, our bartender was friendly and efficient and made sure that our group was taken care of. 

The bar was clean and organized. Even the bathroom was tidy which is a rare occurrence for dive bars. I have only ever ordered beers from here but they do have a full selection for your needs. I like how Tiburon also has a modern jukebox which you can control from an app on your phone. Being able to select songs from the comfort of your seat beats the heck out of feeding money into some dirty machine. 

A good overall spot if you're looking to separate yourself from the craze of the downtown bar scene."}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2019-12-16 06:07:14', 'review_text': "Amazing bar. Ask for for a cocktail if you really want to be treated - that's her specialty!  Will definitely be coming back here when I can!"}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2019-11-24 19:45:32', 'review_text': 'Great local watering hole. Definitely a no-frills dive bar. However, they do have a pool table.'}]}

cases.answer: Cabo's Baja Grill & Cantina, located at 5096 Carpinteria Ave, Carpinteria, California, is a casual Mexican restaurant, good for groups, offering outdoor seating and takeout options, but does not provide Wi-Fi or reservation services. Street parking is available, but there is no garage, validated parking, lot, or valet service. The restaurant has a 3-star business rating. One customer review praised the food, service, and pricing of the new restaurant, Cielo, which replaced Cabo's Baja Grill & Cantina. Another review mentioned a full bar and a variety of food options, but found the steak flatbread overpriced and lacking in seasoning. A third customer was disappointed with the higher prices and poor food quality at Cielo's, reporting a food poisoning incident. The interior and cleanliness of the restaurant were praised, but concerns were raised about the kitchen standards.

contexts.text:
{"address": "5096 Carpinteria Ave", "attributes": {"Ambience": {"casual": true, "classy": false, "divey": false, "hipster": false, "intimate": false, "romantic": false, "touristy": false, "trendy": false, "upscale": false}, "BusinessParking": {"garage": false, "lot": false, "street": true, "valet": false, "validated": false}, "Music": null, "OutdoorSeating": true, "RestaurantsGoodForGroups": true, "RestaurantsReservations": false, "RestaurantsTakeOut": true, "WiFi": "no"}, "business_stars": 3.0, "categories": "Restaurants, Mexican", "city": "Carpinteria", "hours": null, "name": "Cabo's Baja Grill & Cantina", "review_info": [{"review_date": "2015-02-23 02:01:38", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "Loving the new restaurant Cielo in Carpinteria! Looks amazing, food was perfect and great prices! Service was five star and the pricing was 2 star but tasted 5 star!!!!! I'll definitely be back :))"}, {"review_date": "2015-02-21 03:27:48", "review_stars": 3.0, "review_text": "CBG is out of business.  It is now Cielo.  There is a full bar, and the menu is a mix of flatbreads, burgers, tex-mex, assorted appetizers.  There is inside and outside seating.  Bar has 8 spaces so get there early.  I did not have a cocktail so I cannot comment on the bartenders mixing skills. Except I did observe the pop rocks martini...hmmm childhood memories ;)
I had the steak flatbread with onions and cheese.  Crust was paper thin and the steak was good but I was disappointed by a lack of seasoning.  The steak flatbread was overpriced for what you get.  The trip tip plate (tri tip, beans, rice and tortillas ) for 8 dollars looked like a better deal."}, {"review_date": "2015-02-20 22:15:34", "review_stars": 2.0, "review_text": "Today's agenda:  1. Head to Carpinteria  2. Try out the new Cielo's restaurant  3. Get food poisoning

Trust me this was NOT the intention.

My wife and I were so excited when Cielo's finally opened it's doors to the public after weeks of renovations after the take over of Cabo's.  Cabos' was one of our favorite go to spots for lunch and were hoping that Cielo's captured us as a regular the way it's predecessor did.

When we arrived we did notice the positives. The interior was definitely upgraded as the booths give it a more restaurant ambiance.  The bathrooms were nicely done and very clean.

We sat in the front patio overlooking Carpinteria Blvd and took our first look at the new menus.  First thing we notice were the prices were about 25% higher than Cabos which is cool if the food warranted that price.  It's the standard beach diner fare with a bit of a higher end take on it.  Sliders, burgers, chicken plates, salads, apps, etc.   Were were disappointed at the price of the drinks (house margs were $10) and NO pitchers of margs anymore!  That sucked as that was one of the highlights of Cabos.   

First time there we ordered the sliders.  Not bad but not great.  Heavy jalapeno overrode the beefy flavor of the meat.  They had a nice dessert menu and we ordered the carrot cake.  Not bad but the server had no idea where it was sourced from.  

The 2nd time we came we ordered the 1/4 chicken plate as that was our favorite dish at Cabo's.  Nightmare thru and thru.  Our plates arrived and we just looked at each other as the rice was in clumps.  Literally.  The rice and beans were cold to the touch and when we cut into the chicken it was RAW.   Dark pink RAW.  We called over the server and she apologized and took it back.  The same plates were returned with the same pieces of chicken grilled to a near burnt crisp (chefs revenge I'm guessing) and the rice and beans were still cold.  We called over the server to let her know and one of the owners came over to apologize.   We told her that we REALLY want to like this place as we LOVED Cabo's but the standards of the restaurant need to start with the standards in the kitchen and there is just NO care for the food here.  Even the cold beans had no flavor.  I texted my brother that we were here and his reply was "nice patio...terrible food".  

Anyway unfortunately unless there are some major changes in the kitchen I'm never coming back to Cielo's.   Post script the only reason I gave it 2 stars instead of 1 was the server was VERY nice and very helpful"}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer: The Daily Grind is a popular breakfast and brunch spot in Santa Barbara, CA that serves a variety of coffee, tea, sandwiches, and juices. They are known for their delicious bagels and pastries, and the coffee is described as strong and tasteful. The owner is friendly and welcoming, creating a pleasant atmosphere for customers. Some customers have had issues with their orders, such as receiving a salad with a bug in it, but overall, the reviews suggest that the food is good, and the service is generally friendly. The establishment offers outdoor seating, free Wi-Fi, and takes reservations. It's a great spot for casual dining, takeout, and groups. However, some customers have reported poor service from a specific staff member. Despite this, The Daily Grind maintains an overall 3.5-star rating and is considered a solid choice for breakfast and brunch in the area.

cases.answer: Based on the provided structured data, Modern Times Academy of Recreational Sciences is a popular local business in Santa Barbara, California that offers a unique blend of beer, breakfast, brunch, and vegan options. With a rating of 4.5 stars, the business has a loyal customer base who appreciate its welcoming atmosphere, friendly staff, and high-quality products. Customers particularly praise the business's selection of craft beers, including IPAs and barrel-aged stouts, as well as its vegan menu options, which are described as creative, indulgent, and delicious. The business's outdoor patio area is also a highlight, providing a cozy spot for customers to relax and enjoy their meals. While some customers have expressed appreciation for the business's knowledgeable staff, others have noted that the service can sometimes be slow. Nonetheless, Modern Times Academy of Recreational Sciences appears to be a top choice for locals and visitors alike looking for a fun and satisfying dining experience.

cases.answer: Modern Times Academy of Recreational Sciences is a highly rated Beer Bar, Breakfast & Brunch, Vegan, Bars, Nightlife, and Restaurant located in Santa Barbara, CA. The business has a 4.5-star rating and offers a wide variety of beers, including IPAs and barrel-aged stouts, as well as a delicious menu that includes vegan options. The atmosphere is described as casual, trendy, and intimate, with outdoor seating and free WiFi available. The staff is friendly and knowledgeable about beers, and the business is known for its great selection of vegan food. Customers rave about the Munchwrap Supreme, which is similar to a Taco Bell favorite but plant-based, and the Ethereal Embrace, a unique and tasty drink. One customer noted that the girl working in the gift shop was particularly friendly and helpful, making the experience even more enjoyable. Overall, Modern Times Academy of Recreational Sciences is a must-visit spot for anyone looking for great beer, delicious food, and a welcoming atmosphere in Santa Barbara.

cases.answer: 805 Kabob is a Mediterranean restaurant located in Goleta, California. The business operates from Monday to Sunday, 10 AM to 10 PM, and offers street parking, outdoor seating, and takeout services. According to online reviews, the food served at the restaurant is generally good, but the customer service is poor. In particular, some customers have reported being ignored and mistreated by the staff. While the food is priced reasonably, the restaurant can improve its professionalism and avoid long waiting times. Despite these drawbacks, the restaurant does offer a friendly and welcoming atmosphere, which may make it worth visiting for those seeking authentic Middle Eastern cuisine in the Isla Vista area.

cases.answer: American Ale is a nightlife, pubs, beer, wine & spirits, American (traditional) restaurant located at 14 E Cota St in Santa Barbara, CA. The business offers parking, outdoor seating, takeout, and good options for groups. American Ale has a rating of 4.0 stars on Google. The business has a variety of categories, including burgers and bars. Customers have reviewed the business with mixed reviews. One customer stated that they were disappointed with the business and wished it was run better than the food tasted. Overall, American Ale seems like a casual restaurant that serves great food but may need to improve on cleanliness.

cases.answer: Old Town Tavern is a well-rated bar located at 261 Orange Ave, Goleta, CA. This establishment is categorized under Dive Bars, Bars, Nightlife, and Karaoke. It opens daily from 9 am till midnight or later. Street parking is available, and the bar is known for being good for groups and offering outdoor seating. However, it does not offer WiFi or take reservations. Its ambiance leans more towards being 'divey' rather than trendy, hipster, or classy. The tavern has an average star rating of 4.0. Reviews vary, with one customer complaining about the poor service and lack of engagement from a bartender, while others praised the welcoming atmosphere, knowledgeable staff, and the range of liquor available. The Karaoke DJ was also applauded for his attentiveness and willingness to accommodate song requests.

cases.answer:
Old Town Tavern is a local business located at 261 Orange Ave in Goleta, CA. It is categorized as a dive bar, bar, and nightlife venue, with karaoke being one of its main attractions. The bar is open from 9:00 am to 12:00 am every day of the week, offering a variety of drinks and a lively atmosphere.

Customers have left mixed reviews about their experiences at Old Town Tavern. One customer mentioned that the bar was often quiet and that the bartender seemed to be unavailable at times. On the other hand, another customer praised the bartender, Matt Topete, for his quick service and engaging personality. This customer also mentioned that the bar has a wide selection of drinks and a friendly atmosphere.

Overall, Old Town Tavern has received a rating of 4.0 stars. While some customers have had negative experiences with the service, others have enjoyed the bar's offerings and the friendly staff. It is worth noting that the bar has outdoor seating and is suitable for groups. However, there is no information available about the availability of WiFi or takeout options.

In conclusion, Old Town Tavern is a dive bar in Goleta, CA, known for its karaoke nights and lively atmosphere. It has received mixed reviews from customers, with some praising the bartender and the friendly environment, while others have had issues with the service.

cases.answer:
Sushi Tyme is a local restaurant located at 819 State St in Santa Barbara, CA. They specialize in Asian fusion cuisine, seafood, sushi bars, and Japanese dishes. The restaurant is open from 11:00 am to 9:00 pm every day of the week, offering a consistent and convenient dining experience. 

Customers have praised Sushi Tyme for their delicious food and fair prices. One reviewer mentioned that every sushi roll or dish they have tried at the restaurant has been absolutely delicious, giving it a perfect rating of 10/10. Another customer expressed their excitement for the reopening of the store, mentioning the clean and handsome interior. 

Despite facing difficulties due to a fire incident, Sushi Tyme has remained a favorite among customers. The restaurant offers outdoor seating and takeout options, making it suitable for groups. The business has a rating of 3.5 stars. 

Overall, Sushi Tyme is a highly recommended restaurant in Santa Barbara, known for its tasty food, fair prices, and welcoming atmosphere.

cases.answer:
Peabody's is a local business located at 1198 Coast Village Rd in Santa Barbara, CA. It is categorized as a bar, nightlife venue, American (Traditional) restaurant. The business has a rating of 3.5 stars. 

According to customer reviews, Peabody's offers a lively and enjoyable atmosphere. One reviewer shared a positive experience where they met someone special at the bar with the help of friendly bartenders. The staff ensured that their drinks were constantly flowing and even gave subtle signals to help the reviewer make a connection. The reviewer expressed gratitude towards Peabody's for facilitating their relationship and becoming a regular patron.

Another reviewer mentioned that they visited Peabody's after a long day of shopping and sightseeing. They found the food, specifically the calamari and sliders, to be hot, fresh, and tasty. However, the service was not up to their expectations, as they had to personally walk their order to the bartender and refill their own drinks. Eventually, the bartender apologized and offered to buy them a round of drinks and shots, making their evening enjoyable.

On the other hand, one reviewer had a negative experience with Peabody's. They mentioned that they had been to the establishment multiple times and found the food, particularly the pasta, to be consistently disappointing. They also stated that despite having gone on their first date at Peabody's, they had no desire to return.

In summary, Peabody's is a local business in Santa Barbara known for its lively atmosphere and friendly bartenders. While some customers have had positive experiences meeting people and enjoying the food, others have been disappointed with the quality of certain dishes.

{"address": "1198 Coast Village Rd", "attributes": {"Ambience": {"casual": false, "classy": false, "divey": false, "hipster": false, "intimate": false, "romantic": false, "touristy": false, "trendy": false, "upscale": false}, "BusinessParking": {"garage": false, "lot": false, "street": true, "valet": false, "validated": false}, "Music": null, "OutdoorSeating": true, "RestaurantsGoodForGroups": true, "RestaurantsReservations": true, "RestaurantsTakeOut": true, "WiFi": null}, "business_stars": 3.5, "categories": "Bars, Nightlife, American (Traditional), Restaurants", "city": "Santa Barbara", "hours": null, "name": "Peabody's", "review_info": [{"review_date": "2012-05-10 17:48:11", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "The last time I went to Peabody's I had such a good time I just had to write a review.  Here's what happened:  I was sitting at the bar drinking a beer and eating some nachos when this gorgeous girl, Katie, and her friend walked in. They sat next to me and I noticed that Katie had the same phone as me, a crappy freebie phone they give you when you lose your iphone, and so I said, "Hey I like your crappy cell-phone, did you lose your iphone like me?"  As it turned out she had.  We swapped stories about how we had drunkenly lost our phones and after that we just kept talking.  She was making me laugh telling raunchy jokes and I was trying to impress her by weaving beach volleyball stories into the conversation.

The whole time the bartender kept our drinks flowing and made sure we were having a good time.  At one point when the conversation ran dry and I was about to pay my bill and go. Luckily I got the signal from the bartender, "stick around, this girl likes you." I'm hopelessly ignorant to the subtle signals of the opposite sex, and if I hadn't taken his advice I probably would have blown it.  We kept talking for a while and I finally asked her for her number.  Not only did she give me her number, but she walked with me to my car, gave me a kiss on the cheek, flashed me a smile, and made sure I was going to call her.

Needless to say, I did.  We met up a few days later for drinks and we had a great time.  We've been hanging out pretty steadily for a few weeks now and things are going really well.  I had to write a review and thank the guys at Peabody's for hooking me up.  I'm a regular at Peabody's now for sure."}, {"review_date": "2012-03-09 09:42:31", "review_stars": 4.0, "review_text": "My girlfriend and I were staying at the hotel behind this restaurant and decided to try it out after a long day of shopping and sight seeing. After reading some previous comments about service, we were a bit hesitant but decided to roll the dice. We were pleased to find that this place stayed open till midnight on weeknights. We arrived on a Thursday @ 10:30 p.m and were quickly greeted and told to sit anywhere we wanted. We sat next to a warm fire at the fireplace in the lounge but waited a very very long time for someone to take our order........we immediately started talking about the poor service stories we read earlier. After waiting for an eternity, I was forced to walk our order to the bartender (Warren) personally which he promptly took care of. We received our food and drinks in a timely manner and found the calamari and sliders to be hot , fresh, and very tasty. Again, we found ourselves searching for a server to refill our beers but to no avail and ended up walking to the bar for service. Although we found the service to be substandard as we read earlier.....the bartender (Warren) eventually personally came to our table and apologized for us being neglected and without hesitation offered to buy us a round of beers and shots of tequila. With that said, the service was as expected (we did get there @ 10:30 p.m. though) the food was very good, and Warren going above and beyond any other bartender ever has for us made our evening a very enjoyable experience. We highly recommend this restaurant and ask for Warren - he'll take great care of you."}, {"review_date": "2012-01-31 02:06:09", "review_stars": 2.0, "review_text": "ugh.... this place is horrible.
          I have been here many, many times I can honesly cant remember a plate that I ordered that was any good.  Whatever you do, do NOT order any pasta.  Its rubbery and microwaved every single time....
      This is a real bummer because my wife and I went on our first date here and to be totally frank, I still have no desire to ever return.
      This place is totally avoidable...."}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer: Itsuki was a Japanese restaurant located at 7127 Hollister Ave, Ste 30 in Goleta, CA. Specializing in sushi, soups, and ramen, it was open every day of the week from 5:30 PM to 9:00 PM. The restaurant offered outdoor seating and was suitable for groups, although it did not provide Wi-Fi or music. It had a casual ambience and provided lot parking without validation. Unfortunately, as of September 16, 2021, Itsuki has permanently closed. According to customers' reviews, the restaurant was appreciated for its reasonably priced food, but there were mixed reviews about the quality and size of the sushi rolls, as well as the amount of beef in the ramen dishes. The restaurant had an overall rating of 3 stars.

cases.answer:
Café Lido is a local business located at 1111 E Cabrillo Blvd in Santa Barbara, CA. It offers a variety of cuisines including Italian, Mediterranean, and café-style dishes. The café is open from 7:00 am to 8:00 pm on weekdays and from 7:00 am to 9:00 pm on weekends.

Customers have left mixed reviews about their experiences at Café Lido. Some have praised the beautiful ambiance and generous portions of the Crispy Yukon grilled potatoes. However, others have noted issues with the billing process and some dissatisfaction with the food.

One customer mentioned that they were surprised by a 20% service fee added to their bill, which was not mentioned anywhere on the menu or by the servers. This lack of transparency regarding additional charges left a negative impression. Another customer expressed frustration with the mandatory gratuity of 20%, regardless of the quality of service. They suggested that the restaurant should raise prices instead and disclose the mandatory tip on the front door.

On the positive side, some customers have commended the friendly and efficient service provided by the staff. They also mentioned that the lunch and dinner options at Café Lido are good, with one customer recommending the mussels dish.

Overall, Café Lido has a rating of 3.5 stars. While it offers a pleasant dining experience with delicious dishes, transparency regarding additional charges and gratuity policies could be improved.

contexts.text:
{"address": "1111 E Cabrillo Blvd", "attributes": {"Ambience": null, "BusinessParking": null, "Music": null, "OutdoorSeating": true, "RestaurantsGoodForGroups": null, "RestaurantsReservations": null, "RestaurantsTakeOut": true, "WiFi": null}, "business_stars": 3.5, "categories": "Italian, Cafes, Mediterranean, Restaurants", "city": "Santa Barbara", "hours": {"Friday": "7:0-21:0", "Monday": "7:0-20:0", "Saturday": "7:0-21:0", "Sunday": "7:0-20:0", "Thursday": "7:0-20:0", "Tuesday": "7:0-20:0", "Wednesday": "7:0-20:0"}, "name": "Café Lido", "review_info": [{"review_date": "2021-12-14 16:44:15", "review_stars": 2.0, "review_text": "Came here for breakfast since it was open decently early in the morning. The place is beautiful, but some issues with the bill and somewhat the food.

I had both the Crispy Yukon grilled potatoes, and the Lido's breakfast sandwich. The potatoes were delicious, and I found them to be more delicious than the sandwich. The potatoes also had a very generous serving.

The main issue I have, is the 20% service fee that is automatically added to your bill. It's not stated anywhere on the menu, or by the servers. Now I don't mind paying a 20% service fee if I was aware of it, but this restaurant does not notify you about it. Do not be fooled. Add tip on top of this, and you have a fee that's almost 40-50% of your meal. Would not come again."}, {"review_date": "2021-09-10 01:53:26", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "New hours and the system as of Sept 5...now the restaurant is open for breakfast from 7am instead of 8.  Also, you can place your order with a server if you're dining in (instead of lining up at the bar counter).

The catch is...if you would like to dine outdoors, you'd have to wait till 8am when the pool opens.  Also, be aware it gets very busy between 9am and 10:30sh weekends as usual.

All servers are consistently good, friendly, and efficient!

Their lunch and dinner options are good as well.  I was going to have dinner @ Costa, but ended up eating (mussels) here after seeing someone else's meal.  ;)"}, {"review_date": "2021-08-18 17:19:00", "review_stars": 2.0, "review_text": "The food is OK. The granola was not bad. The date shake was so so as well. It really got to me was it took forever for the waiter to come to the table. 

I'm top of that I can't stand companies that demand 20% gratuity regardless of service. I understand if it's a table of six or more etc. or even demanding 15 or 18 percent. But as someone who typically tips over 25% when I am demanded it really bothers me. If this has to do with fair wages then raise the prices of the items. Then you leave it up to the market to decide. But there's nothing posted anywhere that says a mandatory 20% tip. That's my other problem. If you're going to require that we need to disclose it on the front door."}], "state": "CA"}

I had both the Crispy Yukon grilled potatoes, and the Lido's breakfast sandwich. The potatoes were delicious, and I found them to be more delicious than the sandwich. The potatoes also had a very generous serving.

The main issue I have, is the 20% service fee that is automatically added to your bill. It's not stated anywhere on the menu, or by the servers. Now I don't mind paying a 20% service fee if I was aware of it, but this restaurant does not notify you about it. Do not be fooled. Add tip on top of this, and you have a fee that's almost 40-50% of your meal. Would not come again."}, {'review_stars': 5.0, 'review_date': '2021-09-10 01:53:26', 'review_text': "New hours and the system as of Sept 5...now the restaurant is open for breakfast from 7am instead of 8.  Also, you can place your order with a server if you're dining in (instead of lining up at the bar counter).

The catch is...if you would like to dine outdoors, you'd have to wait till 8am when the pool opens.  Also, be aware it gets very busy between 9am and 10:30sh weekends as usual.

All servers are consistently good, friendly, and efficient!

Their lunch and dinner options are good as well.  I was going to have dinner @ Costa, but ended up eating (mussels) here after seeing someone else's meal.  ;)"}, {'review_stars': 2.0, 'review_date': '2021-08-18 17:19:00', 'review_text': "The food is OK. The granola was not bad. The date shake was so so as well. It really got to me was it took forever for the waiter to come to the table. 

I'm top of that I can't stand companies that demand 20% gratuity regardless of service. I understand if it's a table of six or more etc. or even demanding 15 or 18 percent. But as someone who typically tips over 25% when I am demanded it really bothers me. If this has to do with fair wages then raise the prices of the items. Then you leave it up to the market to decide. But there's nothing posted anywhere that says a mandatory 20% tip. That's my other problem. If you're going to require that we need to disclose it on the front door."}]}

Sure! Here's an objective overview of Mediterra Market and Cafe based on the provided structured data:

Mediterra Market and Cafe is a Mediterranean restaurant located in Goleta, CA. The restaurant has a cozy and intimate atmosphere, with outdoor seating available. They offer free WiFi and takeout, and are open seven days a week. According to customer reviews, the restaurant has excellent customer service and the food is delicious, with generous portions and homemade pita bread. One reviewer noted that the menu can be a bit pricey, but the quality of the food makes it worth it. Another reviewer appreciated the European ambiance and the friendliness of the staff. However, one reviewer found the combo plate to be not a good value, as the meat portions were stingy. Overall, Mediterra Market and Cafe seems to be a great option for those looking for authentic Mediterranean cuisine and a welcoming atmosphere.

Itsuki Restaurant is a Japanese restaurant located in the Camino Real Marketplace area of Goleta, near Pacific Oaks. It has a rating of 4.0 stars and offers both dine-in and takeout options. The restaurant has a casual atmosphere and does not offer WiFi or outdoor seating. Its menu includes sushi, sashimi, and rolls, with prices ranging from $15 to $25 per item.

According to customer reviews, the quality of the food at Itsuki Restaurant can be inconsistent, with some reviewers reporting that they were disappointed by the portion size and freshness of the sashimi and rolls. However, other reviewers have praised the restaurant's sushi as being among the best in Santa Barbara, comparable to Arigato. Some reviewers have noted that the restaurant's service can be slow, but others have found it to be satisfactory. Overall, Itsuki Restaurant seems to be a mixed bag, with some customers enjoying their experience while others are disappointed.

cases.answer:
Metropulos Fine Foods is a local business located at 216 E Yanonali St in Santa Barbara, CA. It is a restaurant that also functions as a deli, specialty food store, and grocery. The establishment offers a variety of sandwiches and has a menu that includes soups and salads. Customers have praised the reasonable prices and the quality of the food, describing it as healthy and delicious. The business has a rating of 4.5 stars.

The hours of operation are as follows: Tuesday to Thursday from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM, Friday from 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM, and Saturday from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. The business is closed on Mondays.

Metropulos Fine Foods provides street parking for customers and does not have a parking lot or valet service. It does not accept restaurant reservations and does not have outdoor seating. The business does offer takeout services. As for the ambiance, it is described as casual and not trendy.

Customers have mentioned the limited parking options and the presence of dogs on the patio, which some found intimidating. However, the food is praised for being fresh, tasty, and affordable. The service is generally efficient, although some customers felt rushed. Overall, Metropulos Fine Foods is known for its great sandwiches and is a popular spot for lunch.

{"address": "216 E Yanonali St", "attributes": {"Ambience": {"casual": true, "classy": false, "divey": false, "hipster": false, "intimate": false, "romantic": false, "touristy": false, "trendy": false, "upscale": false}, "BusinessParking": {"garage": false, "lot": false, "street": true, "valet": false, "validated": false}, "Music": null, "OutdoorSeating": null, "RestaurantsGoodForGroups": false, "RestaurantsReservations": false, "RestaurantsTakeOut": true, "WiFi": "no"}, "business_stars": 4.5, "categories": "Restaurants, Delis, Food, Specialty Food, Grocery, Sandwiches", "city": "Santa Barbara", "hours": {"Friday": "9:0-15:0", "Monday": "0:0-0:0", "Saturday": "9:0-16:0", "Thursday": "9:0-16:0", "Tuesday": "9:0-16:0", "Wednesday": "9:0-16:0"}, "name": "Metropulos Fine Foods", "review_info": [{"review_date": "2021-12-21 20:21:19", "review_stars": 4.0, "review_text": "Really reasonable prices and great sandwiches, soups, and salads. We grabbed lunch here while working remotely nearby and looking for something healthy we could eat (everything else that turned up in my search with greater than 4 stars was Mexican - not a surprise!). I had a half turkey sandwich with soup, and the half was a generous size. While they have some trendy drink options, the rest of the place is kind of old school vs trendy (in a good way). Great lunch spot!"}, {"review_date": "2021-11-30 03:36:57", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "Love this place! Great sandwiches. The little mart is really cute. The lady taking the orders kinda rushed me. Never toasted the bread but they got everything else right. The pb and j was crazy drippy. The chips were amazing"}, {"review_date": "2021-11-15 19:40:01", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "The parking is a bit limited, unless you are here for a quick in and out, but there's parking half a block away. I have avoided coming here because I witnessed an unprovoked PitBull attack on the patio, where the PitBull literally took a huge chunk out of a man's leg. We were all confused, and I'm left traumatized by that experience, with a bloody leg imprinted on my mind every time I see this place. Be forewarned: this is a dog place. Big dog, mean dog, and itty bitty dogs come here with their owners. This spot is very popular around lunch time. The food is slow, so you'll have a lot of folks waiting around for carry out during lunchtime. 

If you order here, you will order at the counter and then they give you a number and will bring out your food. I ordered the OMG sandwich which had grilled chicken chunks on a soft roll, along with a chicken salad. You can do half and half! Which is great to be able to order a balanced meal. 

Overall, I'm still scared of the big aggressive dogs that come here but with a little bit of exposure therapy, I can see putting this place into my lunchtime rotation. 

The food is pretty fresh and tasty. The food is affordable. It was worth the wait and....possibly worth the exposure to scary dogs."}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer: Sandpiper Grill is a restaurant located at 7925 Hollister Ave, Goleta, CA. The restaurant serves American traditional cuisine, including sandwiches and salads. According to reviews, the restaurant has a great outdoor patio with an amazing view. Reviewers have praised the freshness and taste of the food, but some have criticized the slow service. Additionally, reviewers have noted that the restaurant can be busy, particularly during golf events at the nearby course. Overall, Sandpiper Grill seems to be a casual restaurant with a focus on fresh, tasty food and great outdoor dining.

{"address": "7925 Hollister Ave", "attributes": {"Ambience": {"casual": false, "classy": false, "divey": false, "hipster": false, "intimate": false, "romantic": false, "touristy": false, "trendy": false, "upscale": false}, "BusinessParking": {"garage": false, "lot": true, "street": false, "valet": false, "validated": false}, "Music": null, "OutdoorSeating": true, "RestaurantsGoodForGroups": true, "RestaurantsReservations": false, "RestaurantsTakeOut": false, "WiFi": "free"}, "business_stars": 3.5, "categories": "Restaurants, American (Traditional)", "city": "Goleta", "hours": {"Friday": "7:0-16:0", "Monday": "7:0-16:0", "Saturday": "7:0-16:0", "Sunday": "7:0-16:0", "Thursday": "7:0-16:0", "Tuesday": "7:0-16:0", "Wednesday": "7:0-16:0"}, "name": "Sandpiper Grill", "review_info": [{"review_date": "2021-06-27 01:05:50", "review_stars": 4.0, "review_text": "Great outdoor patio with truly amazing view. Sandwich and salads are fresh and tasty. Great bargin"}, {"review_date": "2019-06-26 19:15:52", "review_stars": 2.0, "review_text": "Unfortunately, I didn't have a great experience here.  Both times, the service has been incredibly slow.

The last time my SO and I came here, it took nearly an hour for our food to come out, and when it did, they brought me a dish I hadn't ordered.  After almost an hour of waiting for food after we ordered, they had to make what I actually ordered.  This took additional time.  The carnitas quesadilla I ordered was actually good, but there were pieces of bone in the pork.

That morning, there was a golf event scheduled at the course, and there were more people than normal.  However, the grill hadn't brought in more people to cook, so there was only one person taking orders and one person cooking.  It seems really ridiculous today that the grill wouldn't have had the forethought to bring in additional cooks on a day which would most definitely see increased traffic.  Waiting an hour for breakfast is pretty ridiculous."}, {"review_date": "2018-10-20 20:11:23", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "Cafe is open to public (you don't have to be golfing to eat here).
Delicious food! I always get the Ellwood sandwich, hubby always get the patty melt whenever we eat here.
Never had a bad experience!
Beautiful views of the golf course and ocean."}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer:
Here is an objective overview of The Good Bar based on the provided structured data in JSON format:

The Good Bar is a popular bar located inside the Kimpton Hotel in Goleta, California. It offers a wide range of cocktails, craft beers, and wines, with an emphasis on quality and variety. The bar features an inviting atmosphere, with comfortable seating and a hipster-chic decor. The outdoor seating area is also available for guests to enjoy.

According to reviews, The Good Bar is a great spot for groups, with a lively and trendy ambiance. The bar offers free WiFi and does not take reservations, but the wait is usually not too long. The staff is friendly and knowledgeable about their drinks, and the selection is extensive and creative. Some reviewers mention that the prices are a bit high, but the quality of the drinks and the overall experience make it worth it.

One reviewer mentions that the bar can get busy during peak times, and the staff can sometimes be forgetful with orders, but overall, the experience is positive. Another reviewer had a negative experience with the manager, who was rude and unprofessional, but the hotel front desk apologized and offered compensation.

Based on the reviews, The Good Bar is a great spot for those looking for a trendy and upscale bar experience with a wide selection of drinks and a lively atmosphere. However, some may find the prices to be a bit high, and the staff can be forgetful during busy times.

The drinks are a little bit pricey, but the service is good and atmosphere is nice while staying at the hotel."}, {"review_date": "2018-10-03 23:36:43", "review_stars": 4.0, "review_text": "In the ultimate suburbia known as Goleta, Good Bar isn't a bad spot to grab a cocktail. 

I've been here several times since their opening. Trivia nights, work happy hours, nightcaps - Good Bar has hosted it all. I dig the hipster vibe and decor scheme, which is a welcomed change to nearly everywhere else in our little town. 

I enjoy the eclectic cocktail selection on their menu. There are also a select few happy hour drinks and food items at slightly discounted rates, and I believe this bar offers a better happy hour menu than its poolside counterpart. 

This place can get pretty busy during peak times, with hotel guests and locals alike. My main gripe would be on the bar's staffing during these periods. There have been times I've ordered food and was forgotten about. My recommendation would be to come here during an off-time, like later in the evening. Sure, the happy hour will have ended, but the extra tier of service received oddly makes it worth the while. 

I will be back."}, {"review_date": "2018-06-23 07:28:10", "review_stars": 1.0, "review_text": "CLOSED tonight for a private party which is a wedding. I was a hotel guest at the Kimpton Goodland which per the employees is separate from the Outpost and Good Bar. I was a patron as well at the Outpost with a party of 13. Was told to be quite by people from the private party which was a wedding so that their speeches could be heard on the patio which is where they had their dining portion of the reception. Not only did we comply but we shushed others in the restaurant. The wedding guests/party were very appreciative and invited us to dance in the Good Bar where the reception concluded. We went and had a great time dancing until Tyrell the food/beverage manager kicked us out. We kept getting invited in and he kept kicking us out. We did not eat any food or order any drinks, just danced with the party/guests that invited us in. Tyrell kept kicking us out and when we complained to hi. He said "I don't care who invited you in I'm going to keep kicking you out." We told other guests of the wedding we couldn't go in and they said tell the manager to ask the bride/groom who would be fine with us there. We asked Tyrell to do that and he said "No and even if they did say it was ok I still wouldn't let you in." He was very unprofessional and rude. We complained to the hotel front desk and although they offered apologies they also said since they were separate from the restaurant/bar there was nothing they could do. I will NEVER stay at any Kimpton/IHG hotel and once I find out who owns The Outpost and The Good Bar I will never visit their establishments either. Kimpton needs to get this employee under control and teach him about customer service."}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer: Gladden and Sons Produce is a local business located in Santa Barbara, CA that offers a variety of products and services, including organic produce, specialty foods, sandwiches, meats, and coffee. The business has a 4.5-star rating and is known for its support of local farmers and friendly customer service. The store has a cozy atmosphere and offers a convenient delivery service, which has been particularly popular during the COVID-19 pandemic. While some customers have noted that prices can be a bit high, many appreciate the quality of the products and the convenience of the delivery service. The business has implemented safety measures such as requiring staff and customers to wear masks, and has a chalkboard displaying prices near the nuts display. Overall, Gladden and Sons Produce is a well-regarded local business that offers a unique shopping experience and supports the community through its partnerships with local farmers and delivery service.

cases.answer:
Comedy Hideaway is a local business located in Santa Barbara, CA. It offers a variety of services including performing arts, arts & entertainment, caterers, party & event planning, comedy clubs, venues & event spaces, event planning & services, and nightlife. The business operates on Tuesdays from 6:00 PM to 1:00 AM, Fridays from 6:00 PM to 11:00 PM, and Saturdays from 1:00 PM to 11:00 PM.

According to customer reviews, Comedy Hideaway is highly recommended for its great local club atmosphere and rising talent performances. It is described as a hidden gem that provides a unique experience. Some customers mentioned that the venue plans to move to a bigger location in DTSB, indicating potential growth and expansion.

The business has a rating of 4.5 stars, reflecting the positive experiences of its customers. Reviewers appreciated the affordable drinks, plenty of laughs, and the location's proximity to the airport, which adds to its charm. However, some customers mentioned that the seating could be improved.

Overall, Comedy Hideaway offers an enjoyable and fun experience with good food, talented comics, and a casual ambiance. It is a recommended destination for those looking to have a great time and enjoy live comedy shows in Santa Barbara.

cases.answer: Located in Santa Barbara, California, Comedy Hideaway is a hidden gem for comedy enthusiasts. This intimate venue offers a unique experience, featuring rising talent in the comedy scene. With a 4.5-star rating, customers rave about the club's affordable drinks, hilarious performances, and convenient location near the airport. The atmosphere is casual and welcoming, making it an ideal spot for a night out with friends or a date. While the seating could be improved, the free admission makes up for it. According to reviews, the comics are good, and the food is delicious. If you're looking for a laughter-filled evening without breaking the bank, Comedy Hideaway is the perfect spot. Don't miss out on this local treasure before it moves to a larger location in downtown Santa Barbara.

cases.answer:
Epic Bowl is a local business located at 819 B State St in Santa Barbara, CA. It offers a variety of specialty food, breakfast and brunch options, as well as juices and smoothies. The business operates from 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM every day of the week. They have outdoor seating available and offer takeout services. The business parking options include a garage but not street parking, validated parking, a lot, or valet services.

Customers have left mixed reviews about Epic Bowl. One customer mentioned that the variety and size of the bowls were the only impressive aspects, but the taste and customer service fell short. The customer had a disappointing experience with the Rincon bowl and felt that the food lacked flavor. Additionally, the customer service was described as poor, with workers appearing uninterested and unhelpful.

Another customer had a positive experience during their visit. They were on a juice fast and found Epic Bowl to be a great place to fulfill their needs. They highly praised the Superfood Drink and found the staff to be helpful and friendly. They even received the recipe for the drink.

On the other hand, a different customer had a negative experience. They had purchased a Groupon for Epic Bowl but had trouble accessing the business as it was consistently closed during their attempts to visit.

Overall, Epic Bowl has received an average rating of 3.5 stars. While some customers have had positive experiences with the food and service, others have expressed dissatisfaction. It might be worth considering other options before deciding to visit Epic Bowl.

Overview:

cases.answer: Crocodile Restaurant & Bar, located at 2819 State St, Santa Barbara, CA, is a versatile business offering a range of services from American and Italian cuisines, to pizza, breakfast & brunch, bars, and nightlife. Operating daily from 8:30 to 21:30, the business is well-received with an overall rating of 4.0 stars. The establishment offers amenities like free WiFi, outdoor seating, take-out options, and is suitable for group gatherings. Despite not providing street or garage parking, a lot is available for customers. Reviewers praised the friendly staff, spacious and clean dining room, and the quality of food, particularly the pancakes, coffee, and espresso. However, one reviewer noted dissatisfaction with the service and overcooked shrimp. The Crocodile Restaurant & Bar also accommodates late-night workers and received commendation for the staff's accommodating nature.

Overview:

cases.answer: Ca' Dario is a Tuscan-style restaurant located in Santa Barbara, California, offering seafood, wine bars, and Italian cuisine. With a cozy atmosphere and outdoor seating, the restaurant has a 4-star rating and is known for its delicious pasta dishes, bread, and vegetarian options. However, some customers have expressed disappointment with their meals, citing overcooked pasta, lack of flavor, and uninspired salads. The restaurant offers a variety of wines and has a validated parking garage, street parking, and valet services available. Reservations are recommended as the restaurant is quite small. While some customers have had negative experiences, others have enjoyed their meals, particularly the ravioli with sage and brown butter. Overall, Ca' Dario seems to offer a solid choice for those looking for Italian cuisine in Santa Barbara, but may not exceed expectations.

We also have left a few messages for Dario, who never calls

1. Today we ordered a vegetable fasilli and the chicken brick dish. Neither  was very good.  The pasta dish lacked flavor. There wasn't much in terms of vegetables. It was so disappointing

I thought of taking a picture but it really was such a boring dish.  

White beans would give this some flavor and more protein. We've suggested they look at adding some beans to their  vegan vegetarian dishes but have been met with radio silence 

2 The chicken brick dish was not very good. Three people tried it and all were very disappointed. 

I'll try to add a picture of it.  Very little of it was eaten.

3. The bread is an Average plus 

We used to go here years ago and it was pretty good.  I Food today was from the Santa Barbara location on Victoria Street. We've also had disappointing meals At their montecito location.  

We really wish they would spend a little bit more time on their items and their ingredients especially for the pricing.  

Especially given they do not have good vegan options and haven't bothered to get back to us, we think we are done trying.   Ciao Ca Dario"}, {"review_date": "2021-12-18 19:52:22", "review_stars": 3.0, "review_text": "We selected this restaurant because it had an interesting menu with vegetarian options and many positive reviews. In many ways, it offered a solid choice for our party of 10. That said, the overall impression we had was that the food was fairly ordinary. Pasta dishes were served with overcooked pasta and sauces that were unspectacular. Salads were copious, but almost entirely lettuce with lots of shredded cheese. Bread was uninspiring. Only bottled water (not tap) was offered. The one exception was a pork chop that was reportedly quite substantial and cooked exactly right. Wine choices were good, but overpriced compared to other restaurants nearby. Perhaps it was the size of our party, but our overall impression was that the restaurant isn't really trying. At the end of the meal, we had a mixup with the credit cards. Instead of splitting the bill evenly, several of us got credit card receipts for the entire party of 10! This was quickly remedied, but honestly, at this price point, we expected more."}, {"review_date": "2021-12-18 05:32:59", "review_stars": 5.0, "review_text": "I've been hearing about Ca' Dario for ages and planned to go last year until covid shut things down but I finally got the opportunity to go and it was delicious! The aperol spritz was yummy, the caprese salad was great! My favorite dish however was the ravioli with sage and brown butter! The flavors were amazing :-) make a reservation & try it as the restaurant is quite small."}], "state": "CA"}

cases.answer:
El Rincon Bohemio-Goleta is a Mexican restaurant located at 298 Pine Ave in Goleta, California. The restaurant serves Mexican cuisine and is open Monday through Sunday. El Rincon Bohemio-Goleta offers outdoor seating, takeout, and WiFi. The restaurant also allows reservations for larger groups.

According to customer reviews, the restaurant's food quality has decreased recently, with some customers describing it as being the least impressive Mexican food they have had in a while. Others, however, highly praise the restaurant's food, calling it the best in the area.

Some customers also noted that the waitstaff and hostess were friendly and attentive, while others described the ambiance as being casual and trendy. Overall, El Rincon Bohemio-Goleta appears to be a popular spot for Mexican food and drinks in Goleta, with a mix of positive and negative reviews from its customers.

cases.answer: 'The Spot' is a local restaurant situated at 389 Linden Ave, Carpinteria, CA, offering a mix of Mexican and traditional American cuisine, with a special focus on burgers. The restaurant operates from 10:00 am every day, with closing hours varying between 4:00 pm and 8:00 pm depending on the day. It is a casual dining spot that does not accept reservations but offers outdoor seating and is suitable for groups. However, they do not provide WiFi and only accept cash payments, though an ATM is available onsite. 'The Spot' has mixed reviews with an average rating of 3.5 stars, with customers praising the quick service, delicious food, especially the burgers, onion rings, and clam chowder. The restaurant does not provide a parking garage or valet service, but street parking and a parking lot are available.

The Spot is a restaurant located at 389 Linden Ave in Carpinteria, California. The restaurant serves Mexican, American (Traditional), Burgers, and Restaurants cuisine. The hours of operation are Monday through Sunday from 10 am to 7 pm.

The restaurant offers street parking, lot parking, and no validated parking options. However, it does have outdoor seating available. The restaurant does not offer reservations, WiFi, or valet services. They do offer takeout and catering options.

According to customer reviews, the restaurant has a 3.5 out of 5 star rating. Customers highly recommend the onion rings, burgers, clam chowder, fish sandwiches, and other menu items. Many customers praise the restaurant for its casual atmosphere and excellent food quality. However, some customers have noted that the restaurant only accepts cash payments and that there is an ATM available on site. Overall, The Spot appears to be a popular and well-regarded restaurant in the Carpinteria area.

To fold a quilt, follow these steps:

1. Lay the quilt on a flat surface such as a bed or table.
2. Fold the lower right corner up towards the center of the quilt, ensuring the fold is on the bias.
3. Fold the lower left corner up towards the center of the quilt, again on the bias.
4. Fold the top left corner down towards the center of the quilt, again on the bias.
5. Finally, fold the top right corner down towards the center of the quilt, making sure the fold is also on the bias.

cases.answer: You can prevent birds from building nests on your porch by attaching bird netting to the eves around the perimeter of your porch and the top of the porch if it has open rafters. To sync an audiobook from an iPhone to iTunes, you can follow these steps:
1. Launch iTunes on your computer.
2. Connect your iPhone to the computer using a USB cable.
3. In iTunes, click on the name of your iPhone under the Devices section.
4. Select "Books" above the main iTunes window.
5. Choose the audiobooks you want to transfer by clicking on them.
6. Click on "Sync Audiobooks" and select the specific audiobooks you want to transfer, or choose "All Audiobooks" to transfer all of them.
7. Click "Apply" at the bottom of the screen to start the syncing process.
8. After the sync is complete, the audiobooks will be transferred from your iPhone to iTunes.

cases.answer: The passages suggest that trends in India are more typical than those in Vietnam, particularly in the area of education. Vietnam's education system is described as relatively equitable, where poorer children receive the same treatment as their better-off counterparts. In contrast, India's early focus was on getting children into school, which involved setting up schools, and training teachers. Politically, India supported Vietnam's independence from France and opposed American involvement in the Vietnam War. Both countries have a history of colonialism, with India being a former colony of the United Kingdom and Vietnam of France. Post-colonization, India experienced division and conflict among Hindus, Muslims and Sikhs. Cultural and economic links between India and Vietnam date back to the 2nd century.

Based on the given passages, the drawer on the bottom of an oven stove is called a "warming drawer" and is designed to keep hot foods at serving temperature. It is intended to be used for warming food, rather than for storage. Therefore, the correct answer is:

The drawer on the bottom of an oven stove is for warming food.

passage 1:It’s called a “warming drawer,” and it’s where food is supposed to be placed to stay toasty until it’s ready to be eaten! Wikimedia Commons / Brown. As you can see, some models blatantly point this out on their packaging. The stove pictured above has a special label on the drawer.

passage 2:Depending on the model. January 12, 2017. Perhaps, like me, you use that drawer located underneath your stove for storing pans, cookie sheets, and muffin tins. Unfortunately, many of us have been doing it wrong, according to the good people who manufacture stoves. While it does serve as a great spot for all of your back-up cooking tools, on some oven models it's actually meant to be a warming drawer. Check the inside of the drawer or owner's manual, and you may find it says something like this: “The warming drawer is designed to keep hot foods at serving temperature.

passage 3:The bottom drawer on some ovens can be used to broil or warm food. WHAT'S FALSE. Not all ovens have warming drawers, and the bottom drawer on some ovens is used for storage purposes instead. ORIGIN

cases.answer: Based on the given passages, PSA levels refer to the amount of prostate-specific antigen (PSA) present in a man's blood. Elevated PSA levels may indicate prostate cancer, a noncancerous condition such as prostatitis, or an enlarged prostate. The PSA test measures the level of PSA in a man's blood and the results are usually reported as nanograms of PSA per milliliter (ng/mL) of blood. The normal range for PSA levels varies depending on factors such as age and medical history, and different ranges may apply to different individuals. However, according to the American Cancer Society (ACS), 1 total PSA levels between 4.0 ng/ml and 10.0 ng/ml may indicate prostate cancer, benign prostate hyperplasia (BPH), or inflammation of the prostate.

Based on the given passages, here is a brief answer on how to cook a small prime rib roast in the oven:

1. Allow the roast to stand at room temperature for at least 1 hour.
2. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
3. Rub the roast with House Seasoning.
4. Place the roast on a rack in the pan with the rib side down and the fatty side up.
5. Roast for 1 hour.
6. Turn off the oven.
7. While the roast is resting, prepare the au jus by placing the roasting pan on the stovetop over high heat.
8. Add in wine and cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring and scraping any brown bits.
9. Add in beef stock and cook to reduce the juice by half (about 20 minutes).
10. Season the au jus with salt and pepper to taste.

Note: The passages do not provide information on the size or weight of the roast, so cooking times may vary.

cases.answer:
To cook a small prime rib roast in an oven, follow these steps:

1. Allow the roast to sit at room temperature for at least one hour before cooking.
2. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
3. Rub the roast with House Seasoning and place it on a rack in a roasting pan with the rib side facing down and the fatty side facing up.
4. Roast the roast in the oven for one hour.
5. Turn off the oven and let the roast rest for another hour before carving and serving.
6. While the roast is resting, prepare the au jus by placing the roasting pan on high heat and adding in the wine. Cook for eight to ten minutes, stirring and scraping any brown bits. Then add in the beef stock and reduce the juice by half. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

cases.answer:
Based on the given passages, here are the steps to cook a small prime rib roast in the oven:

1. Allow the roast to stand at room temperature for at least 1 hour.
2. Preheat the oven to 375°F (or 450°F according to Passage 2).
3. Rub the roast with House Seasoning (or season with salt and fresh ground black pepper according to Passage 2).
4. Place the roast on a rack in a pan with the rib side down and the fatty side up.
5. Roast for 1 hour (or until desired level of doneness is reached).
6. Turn off the oven (or reduce heat to 350°F and continue to roast for an additional 15-20 minutes per pound for medium-rare, or 20-25 minutes per pound for medium).
7. Let the roast rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving.

It's important to note that the temperature and cooking time may vary depending on the size and thickness of the roast, as well as personal preference for doneness. It's recommended to use a meat thermometer to ensure the roast reaches a safe internal temperature of at least 145°F for medium-rare, 160°F for medium, and 170°F for well-done.

Based on the provided passages, here are some steps for attaching multiple picture frames together:

Passage 1: Table-Stand Grouping
To join two or three frames together, you can use identical frames or one larger frame as the middle piece. Place the frames beside each other to see if you like the effect, and then mark the hinge placement on the connecting sides. Attach two small hinges to the marked areas to join the frames together.

Passage 2: Hanging Multiple Pictures Together
Before attaching the frames together, test different arrangements of the group of multiple pictures on the floor. Once you have decided on the layout, screw the frames to the backer board, repeating the process for each frame. Attach a hanging wire to the back of the frames and hang them on the wall to enjoy the finished result.

Passage 3: Attaching Frames Together
To attach two frames together, place them back to back and mark the hinge placement on the connecting sides. The placement should be the same distance from the bottom edge of each frame to ensure stability when the grouped pictures are placed on a table. Attach two small hinges to the marked areas to join the frames together.

In conclusion, the recommended method for attaching multiple picture frames together involves using identical or matching frames, marking the hinge placement on the connecting sides, and attaching small hinges to join the frames together. It's also important to test different arrangements of the group of pictures on the floor before attaching the frames together, and to ensure that the frames are securely attached to the backer board before hanging them on the wall.

Based on the provided passages, the main causes of necrotizing fasciitis are:

1. Group A beta-hemolytic streptococci (Streptococcus pyogenes): This type of bacteria is the most common cause of necrotizing fasciitis, accounting for around 70% of cases.
2. Other types of bacteria: Passage 3 mentions that several other types of bacteria can also cause necrotizing fasciitis, including staphylococcus.
3. Trauma: Passage 1 states that prior trauma, such as cuts, scrapes, insect bites, burns, or needle puncture wounds, can increase the risk of developing necrotizing fasciitis.

Therefore, the answer to the question is: Group A beta-hemolytic streptococci, other types of bacteria, and trauma.

cases.answer: Chard is a vegetable that has large, fleshy but tender deep green leaves and thick, crisp stalks. It is similar to spinach but has a stronger, more assertive flavor. Fresh young chard can be used raw in salads, while mature chard leaves and stalks are typically cooked or sautéed. Chard is in the same subspecies as beetroot and is cultivated for leaf production. It can be harvested while the leaves are young and tender or after maturity when the stems are slightly tougher. Chard is perishable and should be stored in the refrigerator for a minimum amount of time.

To wash wine glasses properly, follow these steps:
1. Lay a towel in the sink and fill it with hot water.
2. Wash one wine glass at a time to avoid damage.
3. Use a small amount of mild detergent and apply it only to the bottom of the glass and around the rim.
4. Scrub the inside of the glass with a stemware brush that has soft-foam bristles.
5. Rinse the glass thoroughly with hot water.
6. If there is stubborn wine residue, dissolve a small amount of baking soda in hot water and let it stand in the glass for a few minutes.
7. Gently scrub the glass again with the stemware brush.
8. Rinse the glass once more with hot water.
9. Dry the wine glasses with a smooth lint-free cloth, such as a microfiber or flour sack towel.
10. Make sure to dry them completely to avoid any stains or spots.

To fix a lift valve in a toilet, follow these steps:

1. Turn off the water supply valve to the toilet.
2. Remove the old flapper valve by slipping it off the mounting arms.
3. Remove any remaining broken lift chain from the lift arm.
4. Fill the tank by turning on the water valve and then flushing the toilet. Adjust the lift chain as needed by moving the metal clip up or down a few links. Cut off any excess lift chain to prevent tangling.
5. Push the solid end of the toilet lift arm into the flush handle and connect the chain to the third hole on the lift arm. Make sure it is securely fastened.
6. Turn the water valve back on and test out the flush of the toilet. If it flushes slowly, adjust the chain to make it tighter on the lift arm.

passage 2:Step 4: Open the water valve to fill the tank. Flush the toilet to check for correct operation. If needed, readjust the lift chain by moving the metal clip up or down a couple of links. When you’re satisfied, cut off the excess lift chain to prevent it from getting tangled.

passage 3:Push the small solid end of the toilet lift arm into the flush handle. If a screw is included with your part, screw it in tightly. Push it until there is no more give and it is secure. The other end will hang freely in the air. Connect the chain to the third hole on the lift arm. This is the hole closest to the edge of the lift arm. Use the pliers to make sure it is securely fastened. Turn the water valve back on and test out the flush of the toilet. If the toilet flushes slowly, adjust the chain to make it a little tighter on the lift arm.

Sure, here's how to clean the dryer vent based on the given passages:

To clean the dryer vent, you should follow these steps:

1. Locate the dryer vent cover on the outside of your house and remove it.
2. Use a special lint brush with an extendable handle to clean the inside of the pipe.
3. Remove the vent from the back of the dryer by unscrewing the vent clamp and sliding the vent out of the elbow-shaped joint.
4. Clean the vent as well as the hole in the back of the dryer using the lint brush.
5. Reconnect the vent and move the dryer back against the wall.

Additionally, you may also want to remove the bottom front panel of the dryer and clean the area around the lint filter for a more thorough cleaning.

It is important to note that cleaning the dryer vent regularly can prevent dangerous house fires caused by clogged vents.

cases.answer: An adverb clause is a dependent clause that modifies a verb, adjective, or another adverb, while an adjective clause is a dependent clause that modifies a noun or pronoun. Adverb clauses are introduced by subordinate conjunctions, while adjective clauses are introduced by relative pronouns or subordinate conjunctions. The passages provide enough information to answer the question.

contexts.text:
passage 1:An adverb clause is a dependent clause that modifies a verb, adjective or another adverb. It usually modifies the verb. Adverb clauses are introduced by subordinate conjunctions including after, although, as, as if, before, because, if, since, so that, than, though, unless, until, when, where, and while.

passage 2:Parts of the Sentence - Adjective, Adverb, and Noun Clauses. The adjective clause is used to modify a noun or a pronoun. It will begin with a relative pronoun (who, whose, whom, which, and that) or a subordinate conjunction (when and where). Those are the only words that can be used to introduce an adjective clause.

passage 3:Adjective Clauses. Adjective clauses are dependent clauses that modify nouns or pronouns. Much like adverbial clauses, students who are trying to identify adjective clauses should try to determine what kinds of questions the clause in questions answers.

cases.query: difference between an adverb clause and an adjective clause

cases.answer:
Based on the provided passages, the main difference between an adverb clause and an adjective clause is as follows:

* An adverb clause is a dependent clause that modifies a verb, adjective, or another adverb. It is introduced by subordinate conjunctions such as after, although, as, as if, before, because, if, since, so that, than, though, unless, until, when, where, and while. (Passage 1)
* An adjective clause, on the other hand, is a dependent clause that modifies a noun or a pronoun. It is introduced by a relative pronoun (who, whose, him, her, it, that) or a subordinate conjunction (when and where). (Passage 2)

In summary, while both adverb clauses and adjective clauses are dependent clauses, they differ in their function and the words that introduce them. Adverb clauses modify verbs, adjectives, or another adverbs, while adjective clauses modify nouns or pronouns.

cases.answer:
Based on the given passages, here is the proper way to fertilize your grass:

1. Time your fertilization at the beginning of the growing season, which varies depending on the type of grass you have. For warm-season grasses, this is usually in the spring when the grass starts to turn green.
2. Choose a fertilizer based on the type of grass you have and the pH level of your soil. A pH test can help determine the appropriate pH level for your fertilizer.
3. Measure your lawn to determine how much fertilizer to apply.
4. Apply the fertilizer in a perpendicular pattern to ensure even coverage.
5. Check the condition of the grass after applying the fertilizer by stepping on it. If the leaf blades bounce back up, the fertilizer application was sufficient. If they lay flat, you may have over-fertilized.

Therefore, the proper way to fertilize your grass is to time it correctly, choose the right type of fertilizer based on your grass type and soil pH, measure your lawn to determine the amount of fertilizer needed, apply it evenly, and check the condition of the grass after application.
